[[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From Tomb C) from left to right: Tombs D, E, B, A, F}} [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From the east) from left to right: Tombs A, F, C, D, E}} {{Location map|Japan Gunma|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.47|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=34.00|label=Minamishimo Kofun Group|position=top|width=250|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Minamishimo Kofun Group}}}} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|26|24|N|139|0|33|E}} | zoom = 17 | float = right | width = 300| height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Minamishimo Kofun Group}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|22.45|N|139|0|34.03|E}} | label1=Tomb A | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|26|22.40|N|139|0|36.65|E}} | label2=Tomb B | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|26|24.00|N|139|0|29.30|E}} | label3=Tomb C | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|26|25.03|N|139|0|30.87|E}} | label4=Tomb D | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|26|24.40|N|139|0|32.30|E}} | label5=Tomb E | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 }} '''Minamishimo Kofun Group''' (南下古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mounds located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, [[:en:Kitagunma_District,_Gunma|Kitagunma District]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The site has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == The kofun group is situated on the southern slope of a small hill (Ōbayashi-yama) that lies east of Mount Haruna, where small rivers flowing into the Tone River intersect.Minamishimo Kofun Group Pamphlet. Once home to over 100 burial mounds, only nine survive today.Site explanation board. The burial chambers of five mounds (Tombs A, B, C, D, and E) remain open, all of which feature {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}} (horizontal stone chambers). The stone chambers of Tombs A, B, and E are considered particularly significant, showcasing the evolution of stone construction from Kansai-style chambers (Tomb B) to the precise cutting techniques seen in Tombs A and E. Tombs A and E also retain traces of red construction guidelines on their walls, a rare feature found in only three tombs nationwide. The construction dates of the tombs are estimated to span from the mid-6th to late-7th centuries. The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''

[[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From Tomb C) from left to right: Tombs D, E, B, A, F}} [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From the east) from left to right: Tombs A, F, C, D, E}} {{Location map|Japan Gunma|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.47|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=34.00|label=Minamishimo Kofun Group|position=top|width=250|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Minamishimo Kofun Group}}}} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|26|24|N|139|0|33|E}} | zoom = 17 | float = right | width = 300| height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Minamishimo Kofun Group}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|22.45|N|139|0|34.03|E}} | label1=Tomb A | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|26|22.40|N|139|0|36.65|E}} | label2=Tomb B | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|26|24.00|N|139|0|29.30|E}} | label3=Tomb C | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|26|25.03|N|139|0|30.87|E}} | label4=Tomb D | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|26|24.40|N|139|0|32.30|E}} | label5=Tomb E | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 }} '''Minamishimo Kofun Group''' (南下古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mounds located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, [[:en:Kitagunma_District,_Gunma|Kitagunma District]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The site has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == The kofun group is situated on the southern slope of a small hill (Ōbayashi-yama) that lies east of Mount Haruna, where small rivers flowing into the Tone River intersect.Minamishimo Kofun Group Pamphlet. Once home to over 100 burial mounds, only nine survive today.Site explanation board. The burial chambers of five mounds (Tombs A, B, C, D, and E) remain open, all of which feature {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}} (horizontal stone chambers). The stone chambers of Tombs A, B, and E are considered particularly significant, showcasing the evolution of stone construction from Kansai-style chambers (Tomb B) to the precise cutting techniques seen in Tombs A and E. Tombs A and E also retain traces of red construction guidelines on their walls, a rare feature found in only three tombs nationwide. The construction dates of the tombs are estimated to span from the mid-6th to late-7th centuries. The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''
(Ōbayashi Tomb 1)||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1318||Round mound||21 m diameter||colspan=3|Unknown||Unknown|| |} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Minamishimo Kofun Group – Designated September 8, 2023.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/uploaded/attachment/602455.pdf Gunma Prefecture Bulletin, September 8, 2023]}}. == Related Facilities == * Yoshioka Town Cultural Property Center (Yoshioka Town, Minamishimo) – Adjacent to the kofun group. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuya Kofun|ja|三津屋古墳}} * Sōja Kofun Group ''' {{ill|Sōja Atagoyama Kofun|ja|総社愛宕山古墳}} ''' {{ill|Hotoyama Kofun|ja|宝塔山古墳}} ''' {{ill|Janakayama Kofun|ja|蛇穴山古墳}} * {{ill|Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka, Gunma)|ja|三宮神社 (吉岡町)}} == Group] – Yoshioka Town Board of Education {{Coord|36|26|22.47|N|139|0|34.00|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=title|name=Minamishimo Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] The site has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == The kofun group is situated on the southern slope of a small hill (Ōbayashi-yama) that lies east of Mount Haruna, where small rivers flowing into the Tone River intersect.Minamishimo Kofun Group Pamphlet. Once home to over 100 burial mounds, only nine survive today.Site explanation board. The burial chambers of five mounds (Tombs A, B, C, D, and E) remain open, all of which feature {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}} (horizontal stone chambers). The stone chambers of Tombs A, B, and E are considered particularly significant, showcasing the evolution of stone construction from Kansai-style chambers (Tomb B) to the precise cutting techniques seen in Tombs A and E. Tombs A and E also retain traces of red construction guidelines on their walls, a rare feature found in only three tombs nationwide. The construction dates of the tombs are estimated to span from the mid-6th to late-7th centuries. The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''
(Ōbayashi Tomb 1)||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1318||Round mound||21 m diameter||colspan=3|Unknown||Unknown|| |} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic (Ōbayashi Tomb 1)||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1318||Round mound||21 m diameter||colspan=3|Unknown||Unknown|| |} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Minamishimo Kofun Group – Designated September 8, 2023.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/uploaded/attachment/602455.pdf Gunma Prefecture Bulletin, September 8, 2023]}}. == Related Facilities == * Yoshioka Town Cultural Property Center (Yoshioka Town, Minamishimo) – Adjacent to the kofun group. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuya Kofun|ja|三津屋古墳}} * Sōja Kofun Group ''' {{ill|Sōja Atagoyama Kofun|ja|総社愛宕山古墳}} ''' {{ill|Hotoyama Kofun|ja|宝塔山古墳}} ''' {{ill|Janakayama Kofun|ja|蛇穴山古墳}} * {{ill|Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka, Gunma)|ja|三宮神社 (吉岡町)}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minamishimo Kofun Group}} * [http://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E5%8D%97%E4%B8%8B%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4/ Minamishimo Kofun Group] – Yoshioka Town Board of Education {{Coord|36|26|22.47|N|139|0|34.00|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=title|name=Minamishimo Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic sites in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Yoshioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kk]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minamishimo Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minamishimo Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minamishimo Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Minamishimo Kofun Cluster]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minamishimo Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[ja:南下古墳群]]

[[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From Tomb C) from left to right: Tombs D, E, B, A, F}}]] [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From the east) from left to right: Tombs A, F, C, D, E}}]] {{Location map|Japan Gunma|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.47|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=34.00|label=Minamishimo Kofun Group|position=top|width=250|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Minamishimo Kofun Group}}}} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|26|24|N|139|0|33|E}} | zoom = 17 | float = right | width = 300| height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Minamishimo Kofun Group}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|22.45|N|139|0|34.03|E}} | label1=Tomb A | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|26|22.40|N|139|0|36.65|E}} | label2=Tomb B | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|26|24.00|N|139|0|29.30|E}} | label3=Tomb C | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|26|25.03|N|139|0|30.87|E}} | label4=Tomb D | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|26|24.40|N|139|0|32.30|E}} | label5=Tomb E | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 }} '''Minamishimo Kofun Group''' (南下古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mounds located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, [[:en:Kitagunma_District,_Gunma|Kitagunma District]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The site has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == The kofun group is situated on the southern slope of a small hill (Ōbayashi-yama) that lies east of Mount Haruna, where small rivers flowing into the Tone River intersect.Minamishimo Kofun Group Pamphlet. Once home to over 100 burial mounds, only nine survive today.Site explanation board. The burial chambers of five mounds (Tombs A, B, C, D, and E) remain open, all of which feature {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}} (horizontal stone chambers). The stone chambers of Tombs A, B, and E are considered particularly significant, showcasing the evolution of stone construction from Kansai-style chambers (Tomb B) to the precise cutting techniques seen in Tombs A and E. Tombs A and E also retain traces of red construction guidelines on their walls, a rare feature found in only three tombs nationwide. The construction dates of the tombs are estimated to span from the mid-6th to late-7th centuries. The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''

While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''

[[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From Tomb C) from left to right: Tombs D, E, B, A, F}}]] [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From the east) from left to right: Tombs A, F, C, D, E}}]] {{Location map|Japan Gunma|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.47|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=34.00|label=Minamishimo Kofun Group|position=top|width=250|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Minamishimo Kofun Group}}}} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|26|24|N|139|0|33|E}} | zoom = 17 | float = right | width = 300| height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Minamishimo Kofun Group}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|22.45|N|139|0|34.03|E}} | label1=Tomb A | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|26|22.40|N|139|0|36.65|E}} | label2=Tomb B | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|26|24.00|N|139|0|29.30|E}} | label3=Tomb C | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|26|25.03|N|139|0|30.87|E}} | label4=Tomb D | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|26|24.40|N|139|0|32.30|E}} | label5=Tomb E | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 }} '''Minamishimo Kofun Group''' (南下古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mounds located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, [[:en:Kitagunma_District,_Gunma|Kitagunma District]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The site has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == The kofun group is situated on the southern slope of a small hill (Ōbayashi-yama) that lies east of Mount Haruna, where small rivers flowing into the Tone River intersect.Minamishimo Kofun Group Pamphlet. Once home to over 100 burial mounds, only nine survive today.Site explanation board. The burial chambers of five mounds (Tombs A, B, C, D, and E) remain open, all of which feature {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}} (horizontal stone chambers). The stone chambers of Tombs A, B, and E are considered particularly significant, showcasing the evolution of stone construction from Kansai-style chambers (Tomb B) to the precise cutting techniques seen in Tombs A and E. Tombs A and E also retain traces of red construction guidelines on their walls, a rare feature found in only three tombs nationwide. The construction dates of the tombs are estimated to span from the mid-6th to late-7th centuries. The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''
(Ōbayashi Tomb 1)||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1318||Round mound||21 m diameter||colspan=3|Unknown||Unknown|| |} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Minamishimo Kofun Group – Designated September 8, 2023.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/uploaded/attachment/602455.pdf Gunma Prefecture Bulletin, September 8, 2023]}}. == Related Facilities == * Yoshioka Town Cultural Property Center (Yoshioka Town, Minamishimo) – Adjacent to the kofun group. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuya Kofun|ja|三津屋古墳}} * Sōja Kofun Group ''' {{ill|Sōja Atagoyama Kofun|ja|総社愛宕山古墳}} ''' {{ill|Hotoyama Kofun|ja|宝塔山古墳}} ''' {{ill|Janakayama Kofun|ja|蛇穴山古墳}} * {{ill|Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka, Gunma)|ja|三宮神社 (吉岡町)}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minamishimo Kofun Group}} * [http://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E5%8D%97%E4%B8%8B%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4/ Minamishimo Kofun Group] – Yoshioka Town Board of Education {{Coord|36|26|22.47|N|139|0|34.00|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=title|name=Minamishimo Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic sites in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Yoshioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kk]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minamishimo Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minamishimo Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minamishimo Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Minamishimo Kofun Cluster]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minamishimo Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja:南下古墳群]]

Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''

[[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From Tomb C) from left to right: Tombs D, E, B, A, F}}]] [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From the east) from left to right: Tombs A, F, C, D, E}}]] {{Location map|Japan Gunma|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.47|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=34.00|label=Minamishimo Kofun Group|position=top|width=250|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Minamishimo Kofun Group}}}} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|26|24|N|139|0|33|E}} | zoom = 17 | float = right | width = 300| height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Minamishimo Kofun Group}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|22.45|N|139|0|34.03|E}} | label1=Tomb A | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|26|22.40|N|139|0|36.65|E}} | label2=Tomb B | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|26|24.00|N|139|0|29.30|E}} | label3=Tomb C | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|26|25.03|N|139|0|30.87|E}} | label4=Tomb D | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|26|24.40|N|139|0|32.30|E}} | label5=Tomb E | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 }} '''Minamishimo Kofun Group''' (南下古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mounds located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, [[:en:Kitagunma_District,_Gunma|Kitagunma District]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The site has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == The kofun group is situated on the southern slope of a small hill (Ōbayashi-yama) that lies east of Mount Haruna, where small rivers flowing into the Tone River intersect.Minamishimo Kofun Group Pamphlet. Once home to over 100 burial mounds, only nine survive today.Site explanation board. The burial chambers of five mounds (Tombs A, B, C, D, and E) remain open, all of which feature {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}} (horizontal stone chambers). The stone chambers of Tombs A, B, and E are considered particularly significant, showcasing the evolution of stone construction from Kansai-style chambers (Tomb B) to the precise cutting techniques seen in Tombs A and E. Tombs A and E also retain traces of red construction guidelines on their walls, a rare feature found in only three tombs nationwide. The construction dates of the tombs are estimated to span from the mid-6th to late-7th centuries. The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''

[[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From Tomb C) from left to right: Tombs D, E, B, A, F}}]] [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From the east) from left to right: Tombs A, F, C, D, E}}]] {{Location map|Japan Gunma|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.47|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=34.00|label=Minamishimo Kofun Group|position=top|width=250|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Minamishimo Kofun Group}}}} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|26|24|N|139|0|33|E}} | zoom = 17 | float = right | width = 300| height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Minamishimo Kofun Group}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|22.45|N|139|0|34.03|E}} | label1=Tomb A | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|26|22.40|N|139|0|36.65|E}} | label2=Tomb B | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|26|24.00|N|139|0|29.30|E}} | label3=Tomb C | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|26|25.03|N|139|0|30.87|E}} | label4=Tomb D | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|26|24.40|N|139|0|32.30|E}} | label5=Tomb E | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 }} '''Minamishimo Kofun Group''' (南下古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mounds located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, [[:en:Kitagunma_District,_Gunma|Kitagunma District]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The site has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == The kofun group is situated on the southern slope of a small hill (Ōbayashi-yama) that lies east of Mount Haruna, where small rivers flowing into the Tone River intersect.Minamishimo Kofun Group Pamphlet. Once home to over 100 burial mounds, only nine survive today.Site explanation board. The burial chambers of five mounds (Tombs A, B, C, D, and E) remain open, all of which feature {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}} (horizontal stone chambers). The stone chambers of Tombs A, B, and E are considered particularly significant, showcasing the evolution of stone construction from Kansai-style chambers (Tomb B) to the precise cutting techniques seen in Tombs A and E. Tombs A and E also retain traces of red construction guidelines on their walls, a rare feature found in only three tombs nationwide. The construction dates of the tombs are estimated to span from the mid-6th to late-7th centuries. The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''
(Ōbayashi Tomb 1)||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1318||Round mound||21 m diameter||colspan=3|Unknown||Unknown|| |} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Minamishimo Kofun Group – Designated September 8, 2023.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/uploaded/attachment/602455.pdf Gunma Prefecture Bulletin, September 8, 2023]}}. == Related Facilities == * Yoshioka Town Cultural Property Center (Yoshioka Town, Minamishimo) – Adjacent to the kofun group. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuya Kofun|ja|三津屋古墳}} * Sōja Kofun Group ''' {{ill|Sōja Atagoyama Kofun|ja|総社愛宕山古墳}} ''' {{ill|Hotoyama Kofun|ja|宝塔山古墳}} ''' {{ill|Janakayama Kofun|ja|蛇穴山古墳}} * {{ill|Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka, Gunma)|ja|三宮神社 (吉岡町)}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minamishimo Kofun Group}} * [http://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E5%8D%97%E4%B8%8B%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4/ Minamishimo Kofun Group] – Yoshioka Town Board of Education {{Coord|36|26|22.47|N|139|0|34.00|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=title|name=Minamishimo Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic sites in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Yoshioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minamishimo Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minamishimo Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minamishimo Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Minamishimo Kofun Cluster]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minamishimo Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:南下古墳群]]

Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. Tombs A and E also retain traces of red construction guidelines on their walls, a rare feature found in only three tombs nationwide. The construction dates of the tombs are estimated to span from the mid-6th to late-7th centuries. The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''

(Ōbayashi Tomb 1)||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1318||Round mound||21 m diameter||colspan=3|Unknown||Unknown|| |} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Minamishimo Kofun Group – Designated September 8, 2023.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/uploaded/attachment/602455.pdf Gunma Prefecture Bulletin, September 8, 2023]}}. == Related Facilities == * Yoshioka Town Cultural Property Center (Yoshioka Town, Minamishimo) – Adjacent to the kofun group. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuya Kofun|ja|三津屋古墳}} * Sōja Kofun Group ''' {{ill|Sōja Atagoyama Kofun|ja|総社愛宕山古墳}} ''' {{ill|Hotoyama Kofun|ja|宝塔山古墳}} ''' {{ill|Janakayama Kofun|ja|蛇穴山古墳}} * {{ill|Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka, Gunma)|ja|三宮神社 (吉岡町)}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minamishimo Kofun Group}} * [http://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E5%8D%97%E4%B8%8B%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4/ Minamishimo Kofun Group] – Yoshioka Town Board of Education {{Coord|36|26|22.47|N|139|0|34.00|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=title|name=Minamishimo Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic sites in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Yoshioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minamishimo Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minamishimo Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minamishimo Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Minamishimo Kofun Cluster]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minamishimo Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:南下古墳群]]

[[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From Tomb C) from left to right: Tombs D, E, B, A, F}}]] [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From the east) from left to right: Tombs A, F, C, D, E}}]] {{Location map|Japan Gunma|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.47|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=34.00|label=Minamishimo Kofun Group|position=top|width=250|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Minamishimo Kofun Group}}}} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|26|24|N|139|0|33|E}} | zoom = 17 | float = right | width = 300| height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Minamishimo Kofun Group}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|22.45|N|139|0|34.03|E}} | label1=Tomb A | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|26|22.40|N|139|0|36.65|E}} | label2=Tomb B | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|26|24.00|N|139|0|29.30|E}} | label3=Tomb C | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|26|25.03|N|139|0|30.87|E}} | label4=Tomb D | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|26|24.40|N|139|0|32.30|E}} | label5=Tomb E | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 }} '''Minamishimo Kofun Group''' (南下古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mounds located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, [[:en:Kitagunma_District,_Gunma|Kitagunma District]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The site has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == The kofun group is situated on the southern slope of a small hill (Ōbayashi-yama) that lies east of Mount Haruna, where small rivers flowing into the Tone River intersect.Minamishimo Kofun Group Pamphlet. Once home to over 100 burial mounds, only nine survive today.Site explanation board. The burial chambers of five mounds (Tombs A, B, C, D, and E) remain open, all of which feature {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}} (horizontal stone chambers). The stone chambers of Tombs A, B, and E are considered particularly significant, showcasing the evolution of stone construction from Kansai-style chambers (Tomb B) to the precise cutting techniques seen in Tombs A and E. Tombs A and E also retain traces of red construction guidelines on their walls, a rare feature found in only three tombs nationwide. The construction dates of the tombs are estimated to span from the mid-6th to late-7th centuries. The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''
(Ōbayashi Tomb 1)||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1318||Round mound||21 m diameter||colspan=3|Unknown||Unknown|| |} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Minamishimo Kofun Group – Designated September 8, 2023.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/uploaded/attachment/602455.pdf Gunma Prefecture Bulletin, September 8, 2023]}}. == Related Facilities == * Yoshioka Town Cultural Property Center (Yoshioka Town, Minamishimo) – Adjacent to the kofun group. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuya Kofun|ja|三津屋古墳}} * Sōja Kofun Group ''' {{ill|Sōja Atagoyama Kofun|ja|総社愛宕山古墳}} ''' {{ill|Hotoyama Kofun|ja|宝塔山古墳}} ''' {{ill|Janakayama Kofun|ja|蛇穴山古墳}} * {{ill|Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka, Gunma)|ja|三宮神社 (吉岡町)}} == [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From Tomb C) from left to right: Tombs D, E, B, A, F}}]] [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From the east) from left to right: Tombs A, F, C, D, E}}]] {{Location map|Japan Gunma|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.47|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=34.00|label=Minamishimo Kofun Group|position=top|width=250|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Minamishimo Kofun Group}}}} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|26|24|N|139|0|33|E}} | zoom = 17 | float = right | width = 300| height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Minamishimo Kofun Group}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|22.45|N|139|0|34.03|E}} | label1=Tomb A | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|26|22.40|N|139|0|36.65|E}} | label2=Tomb B | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|26|24.00|N|139|0|29.30|E}} | label3=Tomb C | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|26|25.03|N|139|0|30.87|E}} | label4=Tomb D | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|26|24.40|N|139|0|32.30|E}} | label5=Tomb E | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 }} '''Minamishimo Kofun Group''' (南下古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mounds located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, [[:en:Kitagunma_District,_Gunma|Kitagunma District]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The site has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == The kofun group is situated on the southern slope of a small hill (Ōbayashi-yama) that lies east of Mount Haruna, where small rivers flowing into the Tone River intersect.Minamishimo Kofun Group Pamphlet. Once home to over 100 burial mounds, only nine survive today.Site explanation board. The burial chambers of five mounds (Tombs A, B, C, D, and E) remain open, all of which feature {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}} (horizontal stone chambers). The stone chambers of Tombs A, B, and E are considered particularly significant, showcasing the evolution of stone construction from Kansai-style chambers (Tomb B) to the precise cutting techniques seen in Tombs A and E. Tombs A and E also retain traces of red construction guidelines on their walls, a rare feature found in only three tombs nationwide. The construction dates of the tombs are estimated to span from the mid-6th to late-7th centuries. The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''
(Ōbayashi Tomb 1)||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1318||Round mound||21 m diameter||colspan=3|Unknown||Unknown|| |} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From Tomb C) from left to right: Tombs D, E, B, A, F}}]] [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From the east) from left to right: Tombs A, F, C, D, E}}]] {{Location map|Japan Gunma|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.47|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=34.00|label=Minamishimo Kofun Group|position=top|width=250|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Minamishimo Kofun Group}}}} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|26|24|N|139|0|33|E}} | zoom = 17 | float = right | width = 300| height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Minamishimo Kofun Group}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|22.45|N|139|0|34.03|E}} | label1=Tomb A | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|26|22.40|N|139|0|36.65|E}} | label2=Tomb B | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|26|24.00|N|139|0|29.30|E}} | label3=Tomb C | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|26|25.03|N|139|0|30.87|E}} | label4=Tomb D | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|26|24.40|N|139|0|32.30|E}} | label5=Tomb E | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 }} '''Minamishimo Kofun Group''' (南下古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mounds located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, [[:en:Kitagunma_District,_Gunma|Kitagunma District]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The site has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == The kofun group is situated on the southern slope of a small hill (Ōbayashi-yama) that lies east of Mount Haruna, where small rivers flowing into the Tone River intersect.Minamishimo Kofun Group Pamphlet. Once home to over 100 burial mounds, only nine survive today.Site explanation board. The burial chambers of five mounds (Tombs A, B, C, D, and E) remain open, all of which feature {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}} (horizontal stone chambers). The stone chambers of Tombs A, B, and E are considered particularly significant, showcasing the evolution of stone construction from Kansai-style chambers (Tomb B) to the precise cutting techniques seen in Tombs A and E. Tombs A and E also retain traces of red construction guidelines on their walls, a rare feature found in only three tombs nationwide. The construction dates of the tombs are estimated to span from the mid-6th to late-7th centuries. The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''
(Ōbayashi Tomb 1)||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1318||Round mound||21 m diameter||colspan=3|Unknown||Unknown|| |} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic {{Coord|36|26|24.40|N|139|0|32.30|E}} | label5=E号墳 | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 }} '''南下古墳群'''（みなみしもこふんぐん）は、[[群馬県]][[北群馬郡]][[吉岡町]]南下にある[[古墳群]]。群馬県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 群馬県中部、[[榛名山]]東麓において、[[利根川]]に注ぐ小河川（駒寄川・午王頭川）が入り組んで発達した小丘陵（大林山）の南斜面に築造された古墳群である{{Sfn|群馬県の地名|1987}}{{Sfn|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}。一帯にはかつて100基以上南下古墳群パンフレット。（または108基{{Sfn|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}）の古墳が存在したというが、現在は9基が遺存する史跡説明板。。 現在石室が開口するのはA・B・C・D・E号墳の5基で、いずれも埋葬施設を[[横穴式石室]]とする。特にA・B・E号墳は有力墳と位置づけられ、高さのある畿内系石室のB号墳から、截石切組積み石室のA・E号墳への変遷が認められる。またA・E号墳には壁面に石室構築時の赤色作業線が残されており、全国でもA・E号墳と上庄司原4号墳（[[前橋市]]）の3基のみの例として注目される。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]後期-[[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[6世紀]]中葉-[[7世紀]]末頃と推定される。有力古墳の石室が開口状態で遺存しており、石室の規模・構造から石室の発展過程を追える点で貴重な古墳群になる。加えてB号墳→A号墳→E号墳の変遷については、上毛野地域の最有力首長墓群である総社古墳群（前橋市）の[[総社愛宕山古墳|愛宕山古墳]]→[[宝塔山古墳]]→[[蛇穴山古墳]]の変遷との同調関係が注目される{{Sfn|右島和夫|2019}}。ただし南下古墳群の場合には山寄せ古墳を主体とする点で平地古墳を主体とする総社古墳群よりも畿内的要素が強く、また南東には畿内色の極めて強い正八角形墳の[[三津屋古墳]]（吉岡町大久保）も存在することから、総社古墳群を支える南下古墳群勢力と畿内[[ヤマト王権]]による上毛野地域の中央集権化との関係を考察するうえで重要視される古墳群になる{{Sfn|右島和夫|2019}}。 A-F号墳の6基は[[2023年]]（[[令和]]5年）に群馬県指定史跡に指定されている。現在では史跡整備のうえで史跡公園として公開されるが、石室内部への立ち入りは制限されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1993年]]（[[平成]]5年）12月1日、A・B号墳が「南下A号古墳」・「南下B号古墳」として吉岡町指定史跡に指定。 * [[2010年]]（平成22年）3月24日、A-F号墳が「南下古墳群」として吉岡町指定史跡に指定。 * [[2023年]]（[[令和]]5年）9月8日、群馬県指定史跡に指定。 == 一覧 == {| class="sortable wikitable" （大林1号墳）||style="text-align:left"|南下字大林1318||円墳||直径21m||colspan=3|（不明）||（不明）|| |} === A号墳 === [[File:南下A号古墳.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|A号墳 墳丘・石室開口部（2011年）}}]] '''A号墳'''は、丘陵斜面に所在する山寄せの円墳。直径約20-27メートル・高さ約4メートルを測る。埋葬施設は両袖式の横穴式石室で、南方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|右島和夫|2019}}。 * 石室全長：8メートル以上 * 玄室：長さ2.25メートル、幅2.40メートル（奥壁）、高さ2.40メートル * 羨道：長さ4メートル以上 石室壁面の石材は榛名山噴出の角閃石安山岩を加工した截石で、截石切組積みによって構築される{{Sfn|右島和夫|2019}}。壁面には石材加工時の赤色作業線や、漆喰塗布の痕跡が認められる。天井石は榛名山の自然石（山石）の輝石安山岩{{Sfn|右島和夫|2019}}。 築造時期は、古墳時代終末期の7世紀後半頃と推定される。石室の特徴として総社古墳群の宝塔山古墳との共通性が指摘される。 File:Minamishimo Kofun Group-A, genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） File:Minamishimo Kofun Group-A, genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） File:Minamishimo Kofun Group-A, sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） File:Minamishimo Kofun Group-A, sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） === B号墳 === [[File:南下B号古墳.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|B号墳 墳丘・石室開口部（2011年）}}]] '''B号墳'''は、丘陵斜面に所在する山寄せの円墳。直径30約メートル・高さ約6メートルを測る。埋葬施設は両袖式の横穴式石室で、南南西方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|右島和夫|2019}}。 * 石室全長：7.62メートル * 玄室：長さ3.35メートル、幅2.63メートル、高さ3メートル以上 石室の石材は自然石・截石の併用。主に榛名山の自然石（山石）の輝石安山岩で、多くに粗い面加工が認められるほか、大型石材の隙間には河原石の充填が認められる{{Sfn|右島和夫|2019}}。また玄門の門柱には角閃石安山岩の加工石材が使用される{{Sfn|右島和夫|2019}}。壁面の目地・接合部には漆喰状の灰黄褐色粘土を詰める{{Sfn|右島和夫|2019}}。他の古墳と比べて石室高さが抜き出て、玄室前壁の石材も2段積みで構築されるという特徴を示しており、畿内地域の横穴式石室（畿内型石室）が地方に展開した「畿内系石室」の類型に属する{{Sfn|右島和夫|2019}}。 築造時期は、古墳時代終末期の7世紀中頃と推定される。 File:Minamishimo Kofun Group-B, genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） File:Minamishimo Kofun Group-B, genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） === C号墳 === [[File:南下C号古墳.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|C号墳 墳丘・石室開口部（2011年）}}]] '''C号墳'''は、丘陵頂部に所在する円墳。直径約15メートル・高さ約4メートルを測る。墳丘表面では[[円筒埴輪]]片が採集されている。埋葬施設は無袖式の横穴式石室で、東南東方向に開口する。石室の規模は長さ約6.15メートル、幅約1.58メートル（奥壁）、高さ約1.36メートル（奥壁）を測る。石室の石材は自然石で、乱石積みによって構築される。副葬品は詳らかでない。 築造時期は、古墳時代後期の6世紀中葉-後半頃と推定される。南下古墳群のうちでは最古期に位置づけられる。 File:Minamishimo Kofun Group-C, sekishitsu-1.jpg|石室（奥壁方向） File:Minamishimo Kofun Group-C, sekishitsu-2.jpg|石室（開口部方向） === D号墳 === [[File:南下D号古墳.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|D号墳 墳丘・石室開口部（2011年）}}]] '''D号墳'''は、丘陵斜面に所在する山寄せの円墳。直径約13メートル・高さ約3メートルを測る。埋葬施設は両袖式の横穴式石室で、南西方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り。 * 石室全長：約5.63メートル * 玄室：幅約1.5メートル、高さ約1.5メートル 石室の石材は山石・河原石の混用で、乱石積みによって構築される。玄室・羨道の境には1・2段積みによる玄門が構築されるほか、羨道入り口には閉塞石が遺存する。 築造時期は、古墳時代終末期の7世紀前半頃と推定される。 File:Minamishimo Kofun Group-D, genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） File:Minamishimo Kofun Group-D, genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） === E号墳 === [[File:南下E号古墳.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|E号墳 墳丘・石室開口部（2011年）}}]] '''E号墳'''は、丘陵斜面に所在する山寄せの円墳または方墳と推定される。直径（一辺）約17メートル・高さ約2メートルを測る。埋葬施設は両袖式の横穴式石室で、南方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|右島和夫|2019}}。 * 石室全長：4.43メートル以上 * 玄室：長さ2.76メートル、幅1.78メートル、幅2.13メートル（奥壁）、高さ1.71メートル（奥部） * 羨道：長さ1.20メートル以上 石室壁面の石材は榛名山噴出の角閃石安山岩を加工した截石で、截石切組積みによって構築される。壁面には石材加工時の赤色作業線が数多く残るほか、石室各部位の計測値から1尺30センチメートルの唐尺の使用が推測される{{Sfn|右島和夫|2019}}。天井石は榛名山の自然石（山石）の輝石安山岩{{Sfn|右島和夫|2019}}。石室前面には台形状の前庭部の存在が想定される{{Sfn|右島和夫|2019}}。 築造時期は、古墳時代終末期の7世紀末頃と推定される。総社古墳群においても宝塔山古墳から蛇穴山古墳にかけて墳丘・石室が小型化しており、同調した推移が指摘される{{Sfn|右島和夫|2019}}。 File:Minamishimo Kofun Group-E, genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） File:Minamishimo Kofun Group-E, genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） File:Minamishimo Kofun Group-E, genshitsu-3.jpg|玄室壁面の赤色作業線 === F号墳 === [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group-F, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|F号墳 墳丘}}]] '''F号墳'''（大林1号墳）は、円墳。直径約21メートル・高さ約4メートルを測る。埋葬施設は明らかでない。かつて南側の町道工事に際して巨石を除去したと言われ、横穴式石室と推測される。副葬品は詳らかでなく、築造時期も明らかでない。 === その他 === B号墳の北側隣接地において墳丘が認められており、'''G号墳'''と仮称される{{Sfn|右島和夫|2019}}。 == 文化財 == === 群馬県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 南下古墳群 - 2023年（令和5年）9月8日指定{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/uploaded/attachment/602455.pdf 令和5年9月8日群馬県報]}}より群馬県告示第237号 （群馬県ホームページ）。。 == 関連施設 == * 吉岡町文化財センター（吉岡町南下） - 南下古墳群に隣接。 {{Coord|36|26|24.40|N|139|0|32.30|E}} | label5=E号墳 | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 }} '''南下古墳群'''（みなみしもこふんぐん）は、[[群馬県]][[北群馬郡]][[吉岡町]]南下にある[[古墳群]]。群馬県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 群馬県中部、[[榛名山]]東麓において、[[利根川]]に注ぐ小河川（駒寄川・午王頭川）が入り組んで発達した小丘陵（大林山）の南斜面に築造された古墳群である{{Sfn|群馬県の地名|1987}}{{Sfn|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}。一帯にはかつて100基以上南下古墳群パンフレット。（または108基{{Sfn|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}）の古墳が存在したというが、現在は9基が遺存する史跡説明板。。 現在石室が開口するのはA・B・C・D・E号墳の5基で、いずれも埋葬施設を[[横穴式石室]]とする。特にA・B・E号墳は有力墳と位置づけられ、高さのある畿内系石室のB号墳から、截石切組積み石室のA・E号墳への変遷が認められる。またA・E号墳には壁面に石室構築時の赤色作業線が残されており、全国でもA・E号墳と上庄司原4号墳（[[前橋市]]）の3基のみの例として注目される。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]後期-[[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[6世紀]]中葉-[[7世紀]]末頃と推定される。有力古墳の石室が開口状態で遺存しており、石室の規模・構造から石室の発展過程を追える点で貴重な古墳群になる。加えてB号墳→A号墳→E号墳の変遷については、上毛野地域の最有力首長墓群である総社古墳群（前橋市）の[[総社愛宕山古墳|愛宕山古墳]]→[[宝塔山古墳]]→[[蛇穴山古墳]]の変遷との同調関係が注目される{{Sfn|右島和夫|2019}}。ただし南下古墳群の場合には山寄せ古墳を主体とする点で平地古墳を主体とする総社古墳群よりも畿内的要素が強く、また南東には畿内色の極めて強い正八角形墳の[[三津屋古墳]]（吉岡町大久保）も存在することから、総社古墳群を支える南下古墳群勢力と畿内[[ヤマト王権]]による上毛野地域の中央集権化との関係を考察するうえで重要視される古墳群になる{{Sfn|右島和夫|2019}}。 A-F号墳の6基は[[2023年]]（[[令和]]5年）に群馬県指定史跡に指定されている。現在では史跡整備のうえで史跡公園として公開されるが、石室内部への立ち入りは制限されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1993年]]（[[平成]]5年）12月1日、A・B号墳が「南下A号古墳」・「南下B号古墳」として吉岡町指定史跡に指定。 * [[2010年]]（平成22年）3月24日、A-F号墳が「南下古墳群」として吉岡町指定史跡に指定。 * [[2023年]]（[[令和]]5年）9月8日、群馬県指定史跡に指定。 == 一覧 == {| class="sortable wikitable" （大林1号墳）||style="text-align:left"|南下字大林1318||円墳||直径21m||colspan=3|（不明）||（不明）|| |} === A号墳 === [[File:南下A号古墳.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|A号墳 墳丘・石室開口部（2011年）}}]] '''A号墳'''は、丘陵斜面に所在する山寄せの円墳。直径約20-27メートル・高さ約4メートルを測る。埋葬施設は両袖式の横穴式石室で、南方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|右島和夫|2019}}。 * 石室全長：8メートル以上 * 玄室：長さ2.25メートル、幅2.40メートル（奥壁）、高さ2.40メートル * 羨道：長さ4メートル以上 石室壁面の石材は榛名山噴出の角閃石安山岩を加工した截石で、截石切組積みによって構築される{{Sfn|右島和夫|2019}}。壁面には石材加工時の赤色作業線や、漆喰塗布の痕跡が認められる。天井石は榛名山の自然石（山石）の輝石安山岩{{Sfn|右島和夫|2019}}。 築造時期は、古墳時代終末期の7世紀後半頃と推定される。石室の特徴として総社古墳群の宝塔山古墳との共通性が指摘される。 File:Minamishimo Kofun Group-A, genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） File:Minamishimo Kofun Group-A, genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） File:Minamishimo Kofun Group-A, sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） File:Minamishimo Kofun Group-A, sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） === B号墳 === [[File:南下B号古墳.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|B号墳 墳丘・石室開口部（2011年）}}]] '''B号墳'''は、丘陵斜面に所在する山寄せの円墳。直径30約メートル・高さ約6メートルを測る。埋葬施設は両袖式の横穴式石室で、南南西方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|右島和夫|2019}}。 * 石室全長：7.62メートル * 玄室：長さ3.35メートル、幅2.63メートル、高さ3メートル以上 石室の石材は自然石・截石の併用。主に榛名山の自然石（山石）の輝石安山岩で、多くに粗い面加工が認められるほか、大型石材の隙間には河原石の充填が認められる{{Sfn|右島和夫|2019}}。また玄門の門柱には角閃石安山岩の加工石材が使用される{{Sfn|右島和夫|2019}}。壁面の目地・接合部には漆喰状の灰黄褐色粘土を詰める{{Sfn|右島和夫|2019}}。他の古墳と比べて石室高さが抜き出て、玄室前壁の石材も2段積みで構築されるという特徴を示しており、畿内地域の横穴式石室（畿内型石室）が地方に展開した「畿内系石室」の類型に属する{{Sfn|右島和夫|2019}}。 築造時期は、古墳時代終末期の7世紀中頃と推定される。 File:Minamishimo Kofun Group-B, genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） File:Minamishimo Kofun Group-B, genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） === C号墳 === [[File:南下C号古墳.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|C号墳 墳丘・石室開口部（2011年）}}]] '''C号墳'''は、丘陵頂部に所在する円墳。直径約15メートル・高さ約4メートルを測る。墳丘外表では[[円筒埴輪]]片が採集されている。埋葬施設は無袖式の横穴式石室で、東南東方向に開口する。石室の規模は長さ約6.15メートル、幅約1.58メートル（奥壁）、高さ約1.36メートル（奥壁）を測る。石室の石材は自然石で、乱石積みによって構築される。副葬品は詳らかでない。 築造時期は、古墳時代後期の6世紀中葉-後半頃と推定される。南下古墳群のうちでは最古期に位置づけられる。 File:Minamishimo Kofun Group-C, sekishitsu-1.jpg|石室（奥壁方向） File:Minamishimo Kofun Group-C, sekishitsu-2.jpg|石室（開口部方向） === D号墳 === [[File:南下D号古墳.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|D号墳 墳丘・石室開口部（2011年）}}]] '''D号墳'''は、丘陵斜面に所在する山寄せの円墳。直径約13メートル・高さ約3メートルを測る。埋葬施設は両袖式の横穴式石室で、南西方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り。 * 石室全長：約5.63メートル * 玄室：幅約1.5メートル、高さ約1.5メートル 石室の石材は山石・河原石の混用で、乱石積みによって構築される。玄室・羨道の境には1・2段積みによる玄門が構築されるほか、羨道入り口には閉塞石が遺存する。 築造時期は、古墳時代終末期の7世紀前半頃と推定される。 File:Minamishimo Kofun Group-D, genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） File:Minamishimo Kofun Group-D, genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） === E号墳 === [[File:南下E号古墳.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|E号墳 墳丘・石室開口部（2011年）}}]] '''E号墳'''は、丘陵斜面に所在する山寄せの円墳または方墳と推定される。直径（一辺）約17メートル・高さ約2メートルを測る。埋葬施設は両袖式の横穴式石室で、南方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|右島和夫|2019}}。 * 石室全長：4.43メートル以上 * 玄室：長さ2.76メートル、幅1.78メートル、幅2.13メートル（奥壁）、高さ1.71メートル（奥部） * 羨道：長さ1.20メートル以上 石室壁面の石材は榛名山噴出の角閃石安山岩を加工した截石で、截石切組積みによって構築される。壁面には石材加工時の赤色作業線が数多く残るほか、石室各部位の計測値から1尺30センチメートルの唐尺の使用が推測される{{Sfn|右島和夫|2019}}。天井石は榛名山の自然石（山石）の輝石安山岩{{Sfn|右島和夫|2019}}。石室前面には台形状の前庭部の存在が想定される{{Sfn|右島和夫|2019}}。 築造時期は、古墳時代終末期の7世紀末頃と推定される。総社古墳群においても宝塔山古墳から蛇穴山古墳にかけて墳丘・石室が小型化しており、同調した推移が指摘される{{Sfn|右島和夫|2019}}。 File:Minamishimo Kofun Group-E, genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） File:Minamishimo Kofun Group-E, genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） File:Minamishimo Kofun Group-E, genshitsu-3.jpg|玄室壁面の赤色作業線 === F号墳 === [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group-F, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|F号墳 墳丘}}]] '''F号墳'''（大林1号墳）は、円墳。直径約21メートル・高さ約4メートルを測る。埋葬施設は明らかでない。かつて南側の町道工事に際して巨石を除去したと言われ、横穴式石室と推測される。副葬品は詳らかでなく、築造時期も明らかでない。 === その他 === B号墳の北側隣接地において墳丘が認められており、'''G号墳'''と仮称される{{Sfn|右島和夫|2019}}。 == 文化財 == === 群馬県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 南下古墳群 - 2023年（令和5年）9月8日指定{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/uploaded/attachment/602455.pdf 令和5年9月8日群馬県報]}}より群馬県告示第237号 （群馬県ホームページ）。。 == 関連施設 == * 吉岡町文化財センター（吉岡町南下） - 南下古墳群に隣接。 [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From Tomb C) from left to right: Tombs D, E, B, A, F}}]] [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From the east) from left to right: Tombs A, F, C, D, E}}]] {{Location map|Japan Gunma|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.47|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=34.00|label=Minamishimo Kofun Group|position=top|width=250|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Minamishimo Kofun Group}}}} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|26|24|N|139|0|33|E}} | zoom = 17 | float = right | width = 300| height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Minamishimo Kofun Group}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|22.45|N|139|0|34.03|E}} | label1=Tomb A | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|26|22.40|N|139|0|36.65|E}} | label2=Tomb B | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|26|24.00|N|139|0|29.30|E}} | label3=Tomb C | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|26|25.03|N|139|0|30.87|E}} | label4=Tomb D | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|26|24.40|N|139|0|32.30|E}} | label5=Tomb E | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 }} '''Minamishimo Kofun Group''' (南下古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mounds located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, [[:en:Kitagunma_District,_Gunma|Kitagunma District]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The site has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == The kofun group is situated on the southern slope of a small hill (Ōbayashi-yama) that lies east of Mount Haruna, where small rivers flowing into the Tone River intersect.Minamishimo Kofun Group Pamphlet. Once home to over 100 burial mounds, only nine survive today.Site explanation board. The burial chambers of five mounds (Tombs A, B, C, D, and E) remain open, all of which feature {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}} (horizontal stone chambers). The stone chambers of Tombs A, B, and E are considered particularly significant, showcasing the evolution of stone construction from Kansai-style chambers (Tomb B) to the precise cutting techniques seen in Tombs A and E. Tombs A and E also retain traces of red construction guidelines on their walls, a rare feature found in only three tombs nationwide. The construction dates of the tombs are estimated to span from the mid-6th to late-7th centuries. The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''

[[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From Tomb C) from left to right: Tombs D, E, B, A, F}}]] [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From the east) from left to right: Tombs A, F, C, D, E}}]] {{Location map|Japan Gunma|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.47|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=34.00|label=Minamishimo Kofun Group|position=top|width=250|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Minamishimo Kofun Group}}}} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|26|24|N|139|0|33|E}} | zoom = 17 | float = right | width = 300| height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Minamishimo Kofun Group}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|22.45|N|139|0|34.03|E}} | label1=Tomb A | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|26|22.40|N|139|0|36.65|E}} | label2=Tomb B | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|26|24.00|N|139|0|29.30|E}} | label3=Tomb C | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|26|25.03|N|139|0|30.87|E}} | label4=Tomb D | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|26|24.40|N|139|0|32.30|E}} | label5=Tomb E | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 }} '''Minamishimo Kofun Group''' (南下古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mounds located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, [[:en:Kitagunma_District,_Gunma|Kitagunma District]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The site has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == The kofun group is situated on the southern slope of a small hill (Ōbayashi-yama) that lies east of Mount Haruna, where small rivers flowing into the Tone River intersect.Minamishimo Kofun Group Pamphlet. Once home to over 100 burial mounds, only nine survive today.Site explanation board. The burial chambers of five mounds (Tombs A, B, C, D, and E) remain open, all of which feature {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}} (horizontal stone chambers). The stone chambers of Tombs A, B, and E are considered particularly significant, showcasing the evolution of stone construction from Kansai-style chambers (Tomb B) to the precise cutting techniques seen in Tombs A and E. Tombs A and E also retain traces of red construction guidelines on their walls, a rare feature found in only three tombs nationwide. The construction dates of the tombs are estimated to span from the mid-6th to late-7th centuries. The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''
