While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''

[[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From Tomb C) from left to right: Tombs D, E, B, A, F}}]] [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From the east) from left to right: Tombs A, F, C, D, E}}]] {{Location map|Japan Gunma|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.47|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=34.00|label=Minamishimo Kofun Group|position=top|width=250|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Minamishimo Kofun Group}}}} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|26|24|N|139|0|33|E}} | zoom = 17 | float = right | width = 300| height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Minamishimo Kofun Group}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|22.45|N|139|0|34.03|E}} | label1=Tomb A | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|26|22.40|N|139|0|36.65|E}} | label2=Tomb B | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|26|24.00|N|139|0|29.30|E}} | label3=Tomb C | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|26|25.03|N|139|0|30.87|E}} | label4=Tomb D | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|26|24.40|N|139|0|32.30|E}} | label5=Tomb E | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 }} '''Minamishimo Kofun Group''' (南下古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mounds located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, [[:en:Kitagunma_District,_Gunma|Kitagunma District]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The site has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == The kofun group is situated on the southern slope of a small hill (Ōbayashi-yama) that lies east of Mount Haruna, where small rivers flowing into the Tone River intersect.Minamishimo Kofun Group Pamphlet. Once home to over 100 burial mounds, only nine survive today.Site explanation board. The burial chambers of five mounds (Tombs A, B, C, D, and E) remain open, all of which feature {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}} (horizontal stone chambers). The stone chambers of Tombs A, B, and E are considered particularly significant, showcasing the evolution of stone construction from Kansai-style chambers (Tomb B) to the precise cutting techniques seen in Tombs A and E. Tombs A and E also retain traces of red construction guidelines on their walls, a rare feature found in only three tombs nationwide. The construction dates of the tombs are estimated to span from the mid-6th to late-7th centuries. The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''

Once home to over 100 burial mounds, only nine survive today.Site explanation board. The burial chambers of five mounds (Tombs A, B, C, D, and E) remain open, all of which feature {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}} (horizontal stone chambers). The stone chambers of Tombs A, B, and E are considered particularly significant, showcasing the evolution of stone construction from Kansai-style chambers (Tomb B) to the precise cutting techniques seen in Tombs A and E. Tombs A and E also retain traces of red construction guidelines on their walls, a rare feature found in only three tombs nationwide. The construction dates of the tombs are estimated to span from the mid-6th to late-7th centuries. The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''

The site has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == The kofun group is situated on the southern slope of a small hill (Ōbayashi-yama) that lies east of Mount Haruna, where small rivers flowing into the Tone River intersect.Minamishimo Kofun Group Pamphlet. Once home to over 100 burial mounds, only nine survive today.Site explanation board. The burial chambers of five mounds (Tombs A, B, C, D, and E) remain open, all of which feature {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}} (horizontal stone chambers). The stone chambers of Tombs A, B, and E are considered particularly significant, showcasing the evolution of stone construction from Kansai-style chambers (Tomb B) to the precise cutting techniques seen in Tombs A and E. Tombs A and E also retain traces of red construction guidelines on their walls, a rare feature found in only three tombs nationwide. The construction dates of the tombs are estimated to span from the mid-6th to late-7th centuries. The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''

[[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From Tomb C) from left to right: Tombs D, E, B, A, F}}]] [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From the east) from left to right: Tombs A, F, C, D, E}}]] {{Location map|Japan Gunma|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.47|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=34.00|label=Minamishimo Kofun Group|position=top|width=250|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Minamishimo Kofun Group}}}} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|26|24|N|139|0|33|E}} | zoom = 17 | float = right | width = 300| height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Minamishimo Kofun Group}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|22.45|N|139|0|34.03|E}} | label1=Tomb A | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|26|22.40|N|139|0|36.65|E}} | label2=Tomb B | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|26|24.00|N|139|0|29.30|E}} | label3=Tomb C | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|26|25.03|N|139|0|30.87|E}} | label4=Tomb D | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|26|24.40|N|139|0|32.30|E}} | label5=Tomb E | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 }} '''Minamishimo Kofun Group''' (南下古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mounds located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, [[:en:Kitagunma_District,_Gunma|Kitagunma District]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The site has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == The kofun group is situated on the southern slope of a small hill (Ōbayashi-yama) that lies east of Mount Haruna, where small rivers flowing into the Tone River intersect.Minamishimo Kofun Group Pamphlet. Once home to over 100 burial mounds, only nine survive today.Site explanation board. The burial chambers of five mounds (Tombs A, B, C, D, and E) remain open, all of which feature {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}} (horizontal stone chambers). The stone chambers of Tombs A, B, and E are considered particularly significant, showcasing the evolution of stone construction from Kansai-style chambers (Tomb B) to the precise cutting techniques seen in Tombs A and E. Tombs A and E also retain traces of red construction guidelines on their walls, a rare feature found in only three tombs nationwide. The construction dates of the tombs are estimated to span from the mid-6th to late-7th centuries. The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''
(Ōbayashi Tomb 1)||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1318||Round mound||21 m diameter||colspan=3|Unknown||Unknown|| |} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Minamishimo Kofun Group – Designated September 8, 2023.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/uploaded/attachment/602455.pdf Gunma Prefecture Bulletin, September 8, 2023]}}. == Related [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From Tomb C) from left to right: Tombs D, E, B, A, F}}]] [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From the east) from left to right: Tombs A, F, C, D, E}}]] {{Location map|Japan Gunma|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.47|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=34.00|label=Minamishimo Kofun Group|position=top|width=250|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Minamishimo Kofun Group}}}} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|26|24|N|139|0|33|E}} | zoom = 17 | float = right | width = 300| height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Minamishimo Kofun Group}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|22.45|N|139|0|34.03|E}} | label1=Tomb A | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|26|22.40|N|139|0|36.65|E}} | label2=Tomb B | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|26|24.00|N|139|0|29.30|E}} | label3=Tomb C | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|26|25.03|N|139|0|30.87|E}} | label4=Tomb D | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|26|24.40|N|139|0|32.30|E}} | label5=Tomb E | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 }} '''Minamishimo Kofun Group''' (南下古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mounds located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, [[:en:Kitagunma_District,_Gunma|Kitagunma District]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The site has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == The kofun group is situated on the southern slope of a small hill (Ōbayashi-yama) that lies east of Mount Haruna, where small rivers flowing into the Tone River intersect.Minamishimo Kofun Group Pamphlet. Once home to over 100 burial mounds, only nine survive today.Site explanation board. The burial chambers of five mounds (Tombs A, B, C, D, and E) remain open, all of which feature {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chambers). The stone chambers of Tombs A, B, and E are considered particularly significant, showcasing the evolution of stone construction from Kansai-style chambers (Tomb B) to the precise cutting techniques seen in Tombs A and E. Tombs A and E also retain traces of red construction guidelines on their walls, a rare feature found in only three tombs nationwide. The construction dates of the tombs are estimated to span from the mid-6th to late-7th centuries. The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''
(Ōbayashi Tomb 1)||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1318||Round mound||21 m diameter||colspan=3|Unknown||Unknown|| |} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Tombs A and E also retain traces of red construction guidelines on their walls, a rare feature found in only three tombs nationwide. The construction dates of the tombs are estimated to span from the mid-6th to late-7th centuries. The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''

The site has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == The kofun group is situated on the southern slope of a small hill (Ōbayashi-yama) that lies east of Mount Haruna, where small rivers flowing into the Tone River intersect.Minamishimo Kofun Group Pamphlet. Once home to over 100 burial mounds, only nine survive today.Site explanation board. The burial chambers of five mounds (Tombs A, B, C, D, and E) remain open, all of which feature {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chambers). The stone chambers of Tombs A, B, and E are considered particularly significant, showcasing the evolution of stone construction from Kansai-style chambers (Tomb B) to the precise cutting techniques seen in Tombs A and E. Tombs A and E also retain traces of red construction guidelines on their walls, a rare feature found in only three tombs nationwide. The construction dates of the tombs are estimated to span from the mid-6th to late-7th centuries. The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''

[[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From Tomb C) from left to right: Tombs D, E, B, A, F}}]] [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From the east) from left to right: Tombs A, F, C, D, E}}]] {{Location map|Japan Gunma|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.47|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=34.00|label=Minamishimo Kofun Group|position=top|width=250|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Minamishimo Kofun Group}}}} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|26|24|N|139|0|33|E}} | zoom = 17 | float = right | width = 300| height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Minamishimo Kofun Group}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|22.45|N|139|0|34.03|E}} | label1=Tomb A | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|26|22.40|N|139|0|36.65|E}} | label2=Tomb B | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|26|24.00|N|139|0|29.30|E}} | label3=Tomb C | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|26|25.03|N|139|0|30.87|E}} | label4=Tomb D | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|26|24.40|N|139|0|32.30|E}} | label5=Tomb E | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 }} '''Minamishimo Kofun Group''' (南下古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} burial mounds located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, [[:en:Kitagunma_District,_Gunma|Kitagunma District]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The site has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == The kofun group is situated on the southern slope of a small hill (Ōbayashi-yama) that lies east of Mount Haruna, where small rivers flowing into the Tone River intersect.Minamishimo Kofun Group Pamphlet. Once home to over 100 burial mounds, only nine survive today.Site explanation board. The burial chambers of five mounds (Tombs A, B, C, D, and E) remain open, all of which feature {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chambers). The stone chambers of Tombs A, B, and E are considered particularly significant, showcasing the evolution of stone construction from Kansai-style chambers (Tomb B) to the precise cutting techniques seen in Tombs A and E. Tombs A and E also retain traces of red construction guidelines on their walls, a rare feature found in only three tombs nationwide. The construction dates of the tombs are estimated to span from the mid-6th to late-7th centuries. The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''
(Ōbayashi Tomb 1)||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1318||Round mound||21 m diameter||colspan=3|Unknown||Unknown|| |} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Minamishimo Kofun Group – Designated September 8, 2023.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/uploaded/attachment/602455.pdf Gunma Prefecture Bulletin, September 8, 2023]}}. == Related Facilities == * Yoshioka Town Cultural Property Center (Yoshioka Town, Minamishimo) – Adjacent to the kofun group. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuya Kofun|ja|三津屋古墳}} * Sōja Kofun Group ''' {{ill|Sōja Atagoyama Kofun|ja|総社愛宕山古墳}} ''' {{ill|Hotoyama Kofun|ja|宝塔山古墳}} ''' {{ill|Janakayama Kofun|ja|蛇穴山古墳}} * {{ill|Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka, Gunma)|ja|三宮神社 (吉岡町)}} == [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From Tomb C) from left to right: Tombs D, E, B, A, F}}]] [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From the east) from left to right: Tombs A, F, C, D, E}}]] {{Location map|Japan Gunma|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.47|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=34.00|label=Minamishimo Kofun Group|position=top|width=250|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Minamishimo Kofun Group}}}} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|26|24|N|139|0|33|E}} | zoom = 17 | float = right | width = 300| height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Minamishimo Kofun Group}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|22.45|N|139|0|34.03|E}} | label1=Tomb A | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|26|22.40|N|139|0|36.65|E}} | label2=Tomb B | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|26|24.00|N|139|0|29.30|E}} | label3=Tomb C | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|26|25.03|N|139|0|30.87|E}} | label4=Tomb D | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|26|24.40|N|139|0|32.30|E}} | label5=Tomb E | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 }} '''Minamishimo Kofun Group''' (南下古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} burial mounds located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, [[:en:Kitagunma_District,_Gunma|Kitagunma District]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The site has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == The kofun group is situated on the southern slope of a small hill (Ōbayashi-yama) that lies east of Mount Haruna, where small rivers flowing into the Tone River intersect.Minamishimo Kofun Group Pamphlet. Once home to over 100 burial mounds, only nine survive today.Site explanation board. The burial chambers of five mounds (Tombs A, B, C, D, and E) remain open, all of which feature {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chambers). The stone chambers of Tombs A, B, and E are considered particularly significant, showcasing the evolution of stone construction from Kansai-style chambers (Tomb B) to the precise cutting techniques seen in Tombs A and E. Tombs A and E also retain traces of red construction guidelines on their walls, a rare feature found in only three tombs nationwide. The construction dates of the tombs are estimated to span from the mid-6th to late-7th centuries. The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''

[[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From Tomb C) from left to right: Tombs D, E, B, A, F}}]] [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From the east) from left to right: Tombs A, F, C, D, E}}]] {{Location map|Japan Gunma|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.47|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=34.00|label=Minamishimo Kofun Group|position=top|width=250|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Minamishimo Kofun Group}}}} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|26|24|N|139|0|33|E}} | zoom = 17 | float = right | width = 300| height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Minamishimo Kofun Group}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|22.45|N|139|0|34.03|E}} | label1=Tomb A | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|26|22.40|N|139|0|36.65|E}} | label2=Tomb B | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|26|24.00|N|139|0|29.30|E}} | label3=Tomb C | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|26|25.03|N|139|0|30.87|E}} | label4=Tomb D | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|26|24.40|N|139|0|32.30|E}} | label5=Tomb E | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 }} '''Minamishimo Kofun Group''' (南下古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} burial mounds located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, [[:en:Kitagunma_District,_Gunma|Kitagunma District]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The site has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == The kofun group is situated on the southern slope of a small hill (Ōbayashi-yama) that lies east of Mount Haruna, where small rivers flowing into the Tone River intersect.Minamishimo Kofun Group Pamphlet. Once home to over 100 burial mounds, only nine survive today.Site explanation board. The burial chambers of five mounds (Tombs A, B, C, D, and E) remain open, all of which feature {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chambers). The stone chambers of Tombs A, B, and E are considered particularly significant, showcasing the evolution of stone construction from Kansai-style chambers (Tomb B) to the precise cutting techniques seen in Tombs A and E. Tombs A and E also retain traces of red construction guidelines on their walls, a rare feature found in only three tombs nationwide. The construction dates of the tombs are estimated to span from the mid-6th to late-7th centuries. The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''
(Ōbayashi Tomb 1)||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1318||Round mound||21 m diameter||colspan=3|Unknown||Unknown|| |} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Minamishimo Kofun Group – Designated September 8, 2023.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/uploaded/attachment/602455.pdf Gunma Prefecture Bulletin, September 8, 2023]}}. == Related revision.text:
[[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From Tomb C) from left to right: Tombs D, E, B, A, F}}]] [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From the east) from left to right: Tombs A, F, C, D, E}}]] {{Location map|Japan Gunma|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.47|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=34.00|label=Minamishimo Kofun Group|position=top|width=250|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Minamishimo Kofun Group}}}} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|26|24|N|139|0|33|E}} | zoom = 17 | float = right | width = 300| height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Minamishimo Kofun Group}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|22.45|N|139|0|34.03|E}} | label1=Tomb A | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|26|22.40|N|139|0|36.65|E}} | label2=Tomb B | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|26|24.00|N|139|0|29.30|E}} | label3=Tomb C | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|26|25.03|N|139|0|30.87|E}} | label4=Tomb D | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|26|24.40|N|139|0|32.30|E}} | label5=Tomb E | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 }} '''Minamishimo Kofun Group''' (南下古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} burial mounds located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, [[:en:Kitagunma_District,_Gunma|Kitagunma District]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The site has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == The kofun group is situated on the southern slope of a small hill (Ōbayashi-yama) that lies east of Mount Haruna, where small rivers flowing into the Tone River intersect.Minamishimo Kofun Group Pamphlet. Once home to over 100 burial mounds, only nine survive today.Site explanation board. The burial chambers of five mounds (Tombs A, B, C, D, and E) remain open, all of which feature {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chambers). The stone chambers of Tombs A, B, and E are considered particularly significant, showcasing the evolution of stone construction from Kansai-style chambers (Tomb B) to the precise cutting techniques seen in Tombs A and E. Tombs A and E also retain traces of red construction guidelines on their walls, a rare feature found in only three tombs nationwide. The construction dates of the tombs are estimated to span from the mid-6th to late-7th centuries. The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''
(Ōbayashi Tomb 1)||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1318||Round mound||21 m diameter||colspan=3|Unknown||Unknown|| |} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Minamishimo Kofun Group – Designated September 8, 2023.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/uploaded/attachment/602455.pdf Gunma Prefecture Bulletin, September 8, 2023]}}. == Related The burial chambers of five mounds (Tombs A, B, C, D, and E) remain open, all of which feature {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chambers). [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From Tomb C) from left to right: Tombs D, E, B, A, F}}]] [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From the east) from left to right: Tombs A, F, C, D, E}}]] {{Location map|Japan Gunma|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.47|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=34.00|label=Minamishimo Kofun Group|position=top|width=250|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Minamishimo Kofun Group}}}} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|26|24|N|139|0|33|E}} | zoom = 17 | float = right | width = 300| height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Minamishimo Kofun Group}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|22.45|N|139|0|34.03|E}} | label1=Tomb A | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|26|22.40|N|139|0|36.65|E}} | label2=Tomb B | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|26|24.00|N|139|0|29.30|E}} | label3=Tomb C | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|26|25.03|N|139|0|30.87|E}} | label4=Tomb D | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|26|24.40|N|139|0|32.30|E}} | label5=Tomb E | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 }} '''Minamishimo Kofun Group''' (南下古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofuns located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, [[:en:Kitagunma_District,_Gunma|Kitagunma District]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The site has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == The kofun group is situated on the southern slope of a small hill (Ōbayashi-yama) that lies east of Mount Haruna, where small rivers flowing into the Tone River intersect.Minamishimo Kofun Group Pamphlet. Once home to over 100 Kofuns, only nine survive today.Site explanation board. The burial chambers of five mounds (Tombs A, B, C, D, and E) remain open, all of which feature {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chambers). The stone chambers of Tombs A, B, and E are considered particularly significant, showcasing the evolution of stone construction from Kansai-style chambers (Tomb B) to the precise cutting techniques seen in Tombs A and E. Tombs A and E also retain traces of red construction guidelines on their walls, a rare feature found in only three tombs nationwide. The construction dates of the tombs are estimated to span from the mid-6th to late-7th centuries. The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''

[[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From Tomb C) from left to right: Tombs D, E, B, A, F}}]] [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From the east) from left to right: Tombs A, F, C, D, E}}]] {{Location map|Japan Gunma|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.47|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=34.00|label=Minamishimo Kofun Group|position=top|width=250|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Minamishimo Kofun Group}}}} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|26|24|N|139|0|33|E}} | zoom = 17 | float = right | width = 300| height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Minamishimo Kofun Group}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|22.45|N|139|0|34.03|E}} | label1=Tomb A | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|26|22.40|N|139|0|36.65|E}} | label2=Tomb B | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|26|24.00|N|139|0|29.30|E}} | label3=Tomb C | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|26|25.03|N|139|0|30.87|E}} | label4=Tomb D | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|26|24.40|N|139|0|32.30|E}} | label5=Tomb E | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 }} '''Minamishimo Kofun Group''' (南下古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofuns located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, [[:en:Kitagunma_District,_Gunma|Kitagunma District]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The site has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == The kofun group is situated on the southern slope of a small hill (Ōbayashi-yama) that lies east of Mount Haruna, where small rivers flowing into the Tone River intersect.Minamishimo Kofun Group Pamphlet. Once home to over 100 Kofuns, only nine survive today.Site explanation board. The burial chambers of five mounds (Tombs A, B, C, D, and E) remain open, all of which feature {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chambers). The stone chambers of Tombs A, B, and E are considered particularly significant, showcasing the evolution of stone construction from Kansai-style chambers (Tomb B) to the precise cutting techniques seen in Tombs A and E. Tombs A and E also retain traces of red construction guidelines on their walls, a rare feature found in only three tombs nationwide. The construction dates of the tombs are estimated to span from the mid-6th to late-7th centuries. The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''
(Ōbayashi Tomb 1)||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1318||Round mound||21 m diameter||colspan=3|Unknown||Unknown|| |} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From Tomb C) from left to right: Tombs D, E, B, A, F}}]] [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From the east) from left to right: Tombs A, F, C, D, E}}]] {{Location map|Japan Gunma|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.47|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=34.00|label=Minamishimo Kofun Group|position=top|width=250|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Minamishimo Kofun Group}}}} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|26|24|N|139|0|33|E}} | zoom = 17 | float = right | width = 300| height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Minamishimo Kofun Group}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|22.45|N|139|0|34.03|E}} | label1=Tomb A | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|26|22.40|N|139|0|36.65|E}} | label2=Tomb B | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|26|24.00|N|139|0|29.30|E}} | label3=Tomb C | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|26|25.03|N|139|0|30.87|E}} | label4=Tomb D | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|26|24.40|N|139|0|32.30|E}} | label5=Tomb E | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 }} '''Minamishimo Kofun Group''' (南下古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofuns located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, [[:en:Kitagunma_District,_Gunma|Kitagunma District]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The site has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == The kofun group is situated on the southern slope of a small hill (Ōbayashi-yama) that lies east of Mount Haruna, where small rivers flowing into the Tone River intersect.Minamishimo Kofun Group Pamphlet. Once home to over 100 Kofuns, only nine survive today.Site explanation board. The burial chambers of five mounds (Tombs A, B, C, D, and E) remain open, all of which feature {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chambers). The stone chambers of Tombs A, B, and E are considered particularly significant, showcasing the evolution of stone construction from Kansai-style chambers (Tomb B) to the precise cutting techniques seen in Tombs A and E. Tombs A and E also retain traces of red construction guidelines on their walls, a rare feature found in only three tombs nationwide. The construction dates of the tombs are estimated to span from the mid-6th to late-7th centuries. The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''
(Ōbayashi Tomb 1)||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1318||Round mound||21 m diameter||colspan=3|Unknown||Unknown|| |} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Minamishimo Kofun Group – Designated September 8, 2023.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/uploaded/attachment/602455.pdf Gunma Prefecture Bulletin, September 8, 2023]}}. == Related [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From Tomb C) from left to right: Tombs D, E, B, A, F}}]] [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From the east) from left to right: Tombs A, F, C, D, E}}]] {{Location map|Japan Gunma|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.47|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=34.00|label=Minamishimo Kofun Group|position=top|width=250|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Minamishimo Kofun Group}}}} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|26|24|N|139|0|33|E}} | zoom = 17 | float = right | width = 300| height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Minamishimo Kofun Group}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|22.45|N|139|0|34.03|E}} | label1=Tomb A | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|26|22.40|N|139|0|36.65|E}} | label2=Tomb B | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|26|24.00|N|139|0|29.30|E}} | label3=Tomb C | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|26|25.03|N|139|0|30.87|E}} | label4=Tomb D | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|26|24.40|N|139|0|32.30|E}} | label5=Tomb E | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 }} '''Minamishimo Kofun Group''' (南下古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofuns located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, [[:en:Kitagunma_District,_Gunma|Kitagunma District]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The site has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == The kofun group is situated on the southern slope of a small hill (Ōbayashi-yama) that lies east of Mount Haruna, where small rivers flowing into the Tone River intersect.Minamishimo Kofun Group Pamphlet. Once home to over 100 Kofuns, only nine survive today.Site explanation board. The burial chambers of five mounds (Tombs A, B, C, D, and E) remain open, all of which feature {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chambers). The stone chambers of Tombs A, B, and E are considered particularly significant, showcasing the evolution of stone construction from Kansai-style chambers (Tomb B) to the precise cutting techniques seen in Tombs A and E. Tombs A and E also retain traces of red construction guidelines on their walls, a rare feature found in only three tombs nationwide. The construction dates of the tombs are estimated to span from the mid-6th to late-7th centuries. The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''
(Ōbayashi Tomb 1)||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1318||Round mound||21 m diameter||colspan=3|Unknown||Unknown|| |} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Minamishimo Kofun Group – Designated September 8, 2023.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/uploaded/attachment/602455.pdf Gunma Prefecture Bulletin, September 8, 2023]}}. == Related Once home to over 100 Kofuns, only nine survive today.Site explanation board. The burial chambers of five mounds (Tombs A, B, C, D, and E) remain open, all of which feature {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chambers). The stone chambers of Tombs A, B, and E are considered particularly significant, showcasing the evolution of stone construction from Kansai-style chambers (Tomb B) to the precise cutting techniques seen in Tombs A and E. Tombs A and E also retain traces of red construction guidelines on their walls, a rare feature found in only three tombs nationwide. The construction dates of the tombs are estimated to span from the mid-6th to late-7th centuries. The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''

[[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From Tomb C) from left to right: Tombs D, E, B, A, F}}]] [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From the east) from left to right: Tombs A, F, C, D, E}}]] {{Location map|Japan Gunma|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.47|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=34.00|label=Minamishimo Kofun Group|position=top|width=250|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Minamishimo Kofun Group}}}} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|26|24|N|139|0|33|E}} | zoom = 17 | float = right | width = 300| height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Minamishimo Kofun Group}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|22.45|N|139|0|34.03|E}} | label1=Tomb A | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|26|22.40|N|139|0|36.65|E}} | label2=Tomb B | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|26|24.00|N|139|0|29.30|E}} | label3=Tomb C | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|26|25.03|N|139|0|30.87|E}} | label4=Tomb D | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|26|24.40|N|139|0|32.30|E}} | label5=Tomb E | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 }} '''Minamishimo Kofun Group''' (南下古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofuns located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, [[:en:Kitagunma_District,_Gunma|Kitagunma District]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The site has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == The kofun group is situated on the southern slope of a small hill (Ōbayashi-yama) that lies east of Mount Haruna, where small rivers flowing into the Tone River intersect.Minamishimo Kofun Group Pamphlet. Once home to over 100 Kofuns, only nine survive today.Site explanation board. The burial chambers of five mounds (Tombs A, B, C, D, and E) remain open, all of which feature {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chambers). The stone chambers of Tombs A, B, and E are considered particularly significant, showcasing the evolution of stone construction from Kansai-style chambers (Tomb B) to the precise cutting techniques seen in Tombs A and E. Tombs A and E also retain traces of red construction guidelines on their walls, a rare feature found in only three tombs nationwide. The construction dates of the tombs are estimated to span from the mid-6th to late-7th centuries. The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''
(Ōbayashi Tomb 1)||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1318||Round mound||21 m diameter||colspan=3|Unknown||Unknown|| |} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic
