[[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From Tomb C) from left to right: Tombs D, E, B, A, F}}]] [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From the east) from left to right: Tombs A, F, C, D, E}}]] {{Location map|Japan Gunma|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.47|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=34.00|label=Minamishimo Kofun Group|position=top|width=250|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Minamishimo Kofun Group}}}} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|26|24|N|139|0|33|E}} | zoom = 17 | float = right | width = 300| height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Minamishimo Kofun Group}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|22.45|N|139|0|34.03|E}} | label1=Tomb A | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|26|22.40|N|139|0|36.65|E}} | label2=Tomb B | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|26|24.00|N|139|0|29.30|E}} | label3=Tomb C | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|26|25.03|N|139|0|30.87|E}} | label4=Tomb D | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|26|24.40|N|139|0|32.30|E}} | label5=Tomb E | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 }} '''Minamishimo Kofun Group''' (南下古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofuns located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, [[:en:Kitagunma_District,_Gunma|Kitagunma District]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The site has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == The kofun group is situated on the southern slope of a small hill (Ōbayashi-yama) that lies east of Mount Haruna, where small rivers flowing into the Tone River intersect.Minamishimo Kofun Group Pamphlet. Once home to over 100 Kofuns, only nine survive today.Site explanation board. The burial chambers of five mounds (Tombs A, B, C, D, and E) remain open, all of which feature {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chambers). The stone chambers of Tombs A, B, and E are considered particularly significant, showcasing the evolution of stone construction from Kansai-style chambers (Tomb B) to the precise cutting techniques seen in Tombs A and E. Tombs A and E also retain traces of red construction guidelines on their walls, a rare feature found in only three tombs nationwide. The construction dates of the tombs are estimated to span from the mid-6th to late-7th centuries. The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''
(Ōbayashi Tomb 1)||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1318||Round mound||21 m diameter||colspan=3|Unknown||Unknown|| |} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Minamishimo Kofun Group – Designated September 8, 2023.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/uploaded/attachment/602455.pdf Gunma Prefecture Bulletin, September 8, 2023]}}. == Related Facilities == * Yoshioka Town Cultural Property Center (Yoshioka Town, Minamishimo) – Adjacent to the kofun group. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuya Kofun|ja|三津屋古墳}} * Sōja Kofun Group ''' {{ill|Sōja Atagoyama Kofun|ja|総社愛宕山古墳}} ''' {{ill|Hotoyama Kofun|ja|宝塔山古墳}} ''' {{ill|Janakayama Kofun|ja|蛇穴山古墳}} * {{ill|Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka, Gunma)|ja|三宮神社 (吉岡町)}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minamishimo Kofun Group}} * [http://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E5%8D%97%E4%B8%8B%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4/ Minamishimo Kofun Group] – Yoshioka Town Board of Education {{Coord|36|26|22.47|N|139|0|34.00|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=title|name=Minamishimo Kofun Group}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:OSM on jawiki]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic sites in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Yoshioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Minamishimo Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Minamishimo Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:南下古墳群]] {{translated page|ja|南下古墳群|version=103868454|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From Tomb C) from left to right: Tombs D, E, B, A, F}}]] [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From the east) from left to right: Tombs A, F, C, D, E}}]] {{Location map|Japan Gunma|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.47|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=34.00|label=Minamishimo Kofun Group|position=top|width=250|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Minamishimo Kofun Group}}}} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|26|24|N|139|0|33|E}} | zoom = 17 | float = right | width = 300| height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Minamishimo Kofun Group}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|22.45|N|139|0|34.03|E}} | label1=Tomb A | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|26|22.40|N|139|0|36.65|E}} | label2=Tomb B | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|26|24.00|N|139|0|29.30|E}} | label3=Tomb C | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|26|25.03|N|139|0|30.87|E}} | label4=Tomb D | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|26|24.40|N|139|0|32.30|E}} | label5=Tomb E | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 }} '''Minamishimo Kofun Group''' (南下古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} Kofuns located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町|12=draft}}, [[:en:Kitagunma_District,_Gunma|Kitagunma District]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft}}. The site has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == The kofun group is situated on the southern slope of a small hill (Ōbayashi-yama) that lies east of Mount Haruna, where small rivers flowing into the Tone River intersect.Minamishimo Kofun Group Pamphlet. Once home to over 100 Kofuns, only nine survive today.Site explanation board. The burial chambers of five mounds (Tombs A, B, C, D, and E) remain open, all of which feature {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (horizontal stone chambers). The stone chambers of Tombs A, B, and E are considered particularly significant, showcasing the evolution of stone construction from Kansai-style chambers (Tomb B) to the precise cutting techniques seen in Tombs A and E. Tombs A and E also retain traces of red construction guidelines on their walls, a rare feature found in only three tombs nationwide. The construction dates of the tombs are estimated to span from the mid-6th to late-7th centuries. The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''
(Ōbayashi Tomb 1)||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1318||Round mound||21 m diameter||colspan=3|Unknown||Unknown|| |} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Minamishimo Kofun Group – Designated September 8, 2023.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/uploaded/attachment/602455.pdf Gunma Prefecture Bulletin, September 8, 2023]}}. == Related Facilities == * Yoshioka Town Cultural Property Center (Yoshioka Town, Minamishimo) – Adjacent to the kofun group. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuya Kofun|ja|三津屋古墳|12=draft}} * Sōja Kofun Group ''' {{ill|Sōja Atagoyama Kofun|ja|総社愛宕山古墳|12=draft}} ''' {{ill|Hotoyama Kofun|ja|宝塔山古墳|12=draft}} ''' {{ill|Janakayama Kofun|ja|蛇穴山古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka, Gunma)|ja|三宮神社 (吉岡町)|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minamishimo Kofun Group}} * [http://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E5%8D%97%E4%B8%8B%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4/ Minamishimo Kofun Group] – Yoshioka Town Board of Education {{Coord|36|26|22.47|N|139|0|34.00|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=title|name=Minamishimo Kofun Group}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|南下古墳群|version=103868454|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:OSM on jawiki]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic sites in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Yoshioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Minamishimo Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Minamishimo Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:南下古墳群]]

(Ōbayashi Tomb 1)||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1318||Round mound||21 m diameter||colspan=3|Unknown||Unknown|| |} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Minamishimo Kofun Group – Designated September 8, 2023.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/uploaded/attachment/602455.pdf Gunma Prefecture Bulletin, September 8, 2023]}}. == Related Facilities == * Yoshioka Town Cultural Property Center (Yoshioka Town, Minamishimo) – Adjacent to the kofun group. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuya Kofun|ja|三津屋古墳|qq=draft}} * Sōja Kofun Group ''' {{ill|Sōja Atagoyama Kofun|ja|総社愛宕山古墳|qq=draft}} ''' {{ill|Hotoyama Kofun|ja|宝塔山古墳|qq=draft}} ''' {{ill|Janakayama Kofun|ja|蛇穴山古墳|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka, Gunma)|ja|三宮神社 (吉岡町)|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minamishimo Kofun Group}} * [http://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E5%8D%97%E4%B8%8B%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4/ Minamishimo Kofun Group] – Yoshioka Town Board of Education {{Coord|36|26|22.47|N|139|0|34.00|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=title|name=Minamishimo Kofun Group}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|南下古墳群|version=103868454|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:OSM on jawiki]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic sites in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Yoshioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Minamishimo Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Minamishimo Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:南下古墳群]]

revision.text:
[[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From Tomb C) from left to right: Tombs D, E, B, A, F}}]] [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From the east) from left to right: Tombs A, F, C, D, E}}]] {{Location map|Japan Gunma|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.47|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=34.00|label=Minamishimo Kofun Group|position=top|width=250|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Minamishimo Kofun Group}}}} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|26|24|N|139|0|33|E}} | zoom = 17 | float = right | width = 300| height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Minamishimo Kofun Group}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|22.45|N|139|0|34.03|E}} | label1=Tomb A | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|26|22.40|N|139|0|36.65|E}} | label2=Tomb B | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|26|24.00|N|139|0|29.30|E}} | label3=Tomb C | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|26|25.03|N|139|0|30.87|E}} | label4=Tomb D | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|26|24.40|N|139|0|32.30|E}} | label5=Tomb E | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 }} '''Minamishimo Kofun Group''' (南下古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} Kofuns located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka, Gunma|qq=}}, [[:en:Kitagunma_District,_Gunma|Kitagunma District]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefecture|qq=}}. The site has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == The kofun group is situated on the southern slope of a small hill (Ōbayashi-yama) that lies east of Mount Haruna, where small rivers flowing into the Tone River intersect.Minamishimo Kofun Group Pamphlet. Once home to over 100 Kofuns, only nine survive today.Site explanation board. The burial chambers of five mounds (Tombs A, B, C, D, and E) remain open, all of which feature {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chambers). The stone chambers of Tombs A, B, and E are considered particularly significant, showcasing the evolution of stone construction from Kansai-style chambers (Tomb B) to the precise cutting techniques seen in Tombs A and E. Tombs A and E also retain traces of red construction guidelines on their walls, a rare feature found in only three tombs nationwide. The construction dates of the tombs are estimated to span from the mid-6th to late-7th centuries. The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''
(Ōbayashi Tomb 1)||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1318||Round mound||21 m diameter||colspan=3|Unknown||Unknown|| |} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Minamishimo Kofun Group – Designated September 8, 2023.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/uploaded/attachment/602455.pdf Gunma Prefecture Bulletin, September 8, 2023]}}. == Related Facilities == * Yoshioka Town Cultural Property Center (Yoshioka Town, Minamishimo) – Adjacent to the kofun group. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuya Kofun|ja|三津屋古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuya Kofun|qq=}} * Sōja Kofun Group ''' {{ill|Sōja Atagoyama Kofun|ja|総社愛宕山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja Atagoyama Kofun|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Hotoyama Kofun|ja|宝塔山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotoyama Kofun|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Janakayama Kofun|ja|蛇穴山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Janakayama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka, Gunma)|ja|三宮神社 (吉岡町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka, Gunma)|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minamishimo Kofun Group}} * [http://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E5%8D%97%E4%B8%8B%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4/ Minamishimo Kofun Group] – Yoshioka Town Board of Education {{Coord|36|26|22.47|N|139|0|34.00|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=title|name=Minamishimo Kofun Group}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|南下古墳群|version=103868454|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:OSM on jawiki]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic sites in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Yoshioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Minamishimo Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Minamishimo Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:南下古墳群]]

The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''
(Ōbayashi Tomb 1)||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1318||Round mound||21 m diameter||colspan=3|Unknown||Unknown|| |} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Minamishimo Kofun Group – Designated September 8, 2023.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/uploaded/attachment/602455.pdf Gunma Prefecture Bulletin, September 8, 2023]}}. == Related Facilities == * Yoshioka Town Cultural Property Center (Yoshioka Town, Minamishimo) – Adjacent to the kofun group. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuya Kofun|ja|三津屋古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuya Kofun|qq=}} * Sōja Kofun Group ''' {{ill|Sōja Atagoyama Kofun|ja|総社愛宕山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja Atagoyama Kofun|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Hotoyama Kofun|ja|宝塔山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotoyama Kofun|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Janakayama Kofun|ja|蛇穴山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Janakayama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka Town)|lt=Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka Town)|lt=Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka, Gunma)|ja|三宮神社 (吉岡町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka, Gunma)|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minamishimo Kofun Group}} * [http://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E5%8D%97%E4%B8%8B%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4/ Minamishimo Kofun Group] – Yoshioka Town Board of Education {{Coord|36|26|22.47|N|139|0|34.00|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=title|name=Minamishimo Kofun Group}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|南下古墳群|version=103868454|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:OSM on jawiki]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic sites in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Yoshioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Minamishimo Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:南下古墳群]]

revision.text:
[[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From Tomb C) from left to right: Tombs D, E, B, A, F}}]] [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From the east) from left to right: Tombs A, F, C, D, E}}]] {{Location map|Japan Gunma|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.47|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=34.00|label=Minamishimo Kofun Group|position=top|width=250|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Minamishimo Kofun Group}}}} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|26|24|N|139|0|33|E}} | zoom = 17 | float = right | width = 300| height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Minamishimo Kofun Group}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|22.45|N|139|0|34.03|E}} | label1=Tomb A | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|26|22.40|N|139|0|36.65|E}} | label2=Tomb B | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|26|24.00|N|139|0|29.30|E}} | label3=Tomb C | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|26|25.03|N|139|0|30.87|E}} | label4=Tomb D | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|26|24.40|N|139|0|32.30|E}} | label5=Tomb E | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 }} '''Minamishimo Kofun Group''' (南下古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Yoshioka, Gunma|Yoshioka, Gunma]], [[:en:Kitagunma_District,_Gunma|Kitagunma District]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. The site has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == The kofun group is situated on the southern slope of a small hill (Ōbayashi-yama) that lies east of Mount Haruna, where small rivers flowing into the Tone River intersect.Minamishimo Kofun Group Pamphlet. Once home to over 100 Kofuns, only nine survive today.Site explanation board. The burial chambers of five mounds (Tombs A, B, C, D, and E) remain open, all of which feature {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chambers). The stone chambers of Tombs A, B, and E are considered particularly significant, showcasing the evolution of stone construction from Kansai-style chambers (Tomb B) to the precise cutting techniques seen in Tombs A and E. Tombs A and E also retain traces of red construction guidelines on their walls, a rare feature found in only three tombs nationwide. The construction dates of the tombs are estimated to span from the mid-6th to late-7th centuries. The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''
(Ōbayashi Tomb 1)||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1318||Round mound||21 m diameter||colspan=3|Unknown||Unknown|| |} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Minamishimo Kofun Group – Designated September 8, 2023.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/uploaded/attachment/602455.pdf Gunma Prefecture Bulletin, September 8, 2023]}}. == Related Facilities == * Yoshioka Town Cultural Property Center (Yoshioka Town, Minamishimo) – Adjacent to the kofun group. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuya Kofun|ja|三津屋古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuya Kofun|qq=}} * Sōja Kofun Group ''' {{ill|Sōja Atagoyama Kofun|ja|総社愛宕山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja Atagoyama Kofun|qq=}} ''' [[:en:Hōtōzan Kofun|Hotoyama Kofun]] ''' [[:en:Jaketsuzan Kofun|Janakayama Kofun]] * {{ill|Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka Town)|lt=Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka Town)|lt=Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka, Gunma)|ja|三宮神社 (吉岡町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka, Gunma)|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minamishimo Kofun Group}} * [http://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E5%8D%97%E4%B8%8B%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4/ Minamishimo Kofun Group] – Yoshioka Town Board of Education {{Coord|36|26|22.47|N|139|0|34.00|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=title|name=Minamishimo Kofun Group}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|南下古墳群|version=103868454|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:OSM on jawiki]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic sites in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Yoshioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Minamishimo Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:南下古墳群]]

While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''
(Ōbayashi Tomb 1)||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1318||Round mound||21 m diameter||colspan=3|Unknown||Unknown|| |} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Minamishimo Kofun Group – Designated September 8, 2023.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/uploaded/attachment/602455.pdf Gunma Prefecture Bulletin, September 8, 2023]}}. == Related Facilities == * Yoshioka Town Cultural Property Center (Yoshioka Town, Minamishimo) – Adjacent to the kofun group. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuya Kofun|ja|三津屋古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuya Kofun|qq=}} * Sōja Kofun Group ''' {{ill|Sōja Atagoyama Kofun|ja|総社愛宕山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja Atagoyama Kofun|qq=}} ''' [[:en:Hōtōzan Kofun|Hotoyama Kofun]] ''' [[:en:Jaketsuzan Kofun|Janakayama Kofun]] * {{ill|Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka Town)|lt=Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka Town)|lt=Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka, Gunma)|ja|三宮神社 (吉岡町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka, Gunma)|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minamishimo Kofun Group}} * [http://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E5%8D%97%E4%B8%8B%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4/ Minamishimo Kofun Group] – Yoshioka Town Board of Education {{Coord|36|26|22.47|N|139|0|34.00|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=title|name=Minamishimo Kofun Group}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|南下古墳群|version=103868454|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:OSM on jawiki]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic sites in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Yoshioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Minamishimo Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:南下古墳群]] [[Category:吉岡町の歴史]] [[Category:群馬県の古墳]]

revision.text:
[[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From Tomb C) from left to right: Tombs D, E, B, A, F}}]] [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From the east) from left to right: Tombs A, F, C, D, E}}]] {{Location map|Japan Gunma|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.47|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=34.00|label=Minamishimo Kofun Group|position=top|width=250|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Minamishimo Kofun Group}}}} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|26|24|N|139|0|33|E}} | zoom = 17 | float = right | width = 300| height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Minamishimo Kofun Group}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|22.45|N|139|0|34.03|E}} | label1=Tomb A | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|26|22.40|N|139|0|36.65|E}} | label2=Tomb B | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|26|24.00|N|139|0|29.30|E}} | label3=Tomb C | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|26|25.03|N|139|0|30.87|E}} | label4=Tomb D | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|26|24.40|N|139|0|32.30|E}} | label5=Tomb E | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 }} '''Minamishimo Kofun Group''' (南下古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Yoshioka, Gunma|Yoshioka, Gunma]], [[:en:Kitagunma_District,_Gunma|Kitagunma District]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. The site has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == The kofun group is situated on the southern slope of a small hill (Ōbayashi-yama) that lies east of Mount Haruna, where small rivers flowing into the Tone River intersect.Minamishimo Kofun Group Pamphlet. Once home to over 100 Kofuns, only nine survive today.Site explanation board. The burial chambers of five mounds (Tombs A, B, C, D, and E) remain open, all of which feature {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chambers). The stone chambers of Tombs A, B, and E are considered particularly significant, showcasing the evolution of stone construction from Kansai-style chambers (Tomb B) to the precise cutting techniques seen in Tombs A and E. Tombs A and E also retain traces of red construction guidelines on their walls, a rare feature found in only three tombs nationwide. The construction dates of the tombs are estimated to span from the mid-6th to late-7th centuries. The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''
(Ōbayashi Tomb 1)||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1318||Round mound||21 m diameter||colspan=3|Unknown||Unknown|| |} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Minamishimo Kofun Group – Designated September 8, 2023.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/uploaded/attachment/602455.pdf Gunma Prefecture Bulletin, September 8, 2023]}}. == Related Facilities == * Yoshioka Town Cultural Property Center (Yoshioka Town, Minamishimo) – Adjacent to the kofun group. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuya Kofun|ja|三津屋古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuya Kofun|qq=}} * Sōja Kofun Group ''' {{ill|Sōja Atagoyama Kofun|ja|総社愛宕山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja Atagoyama Kofun|qq=}} ''' [[:en:Hōtōzan Kofun|Hotoyama Kofun]] ''' [[:en:Jaketsuzan Kofun|Janakayama Kofun]] * {{ill|Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka Town)|lt=Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka Town)|lt=Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka, Gunma)|ja|三宮神社 (吉岡町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka, Gunma)|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minamishimo Kofun Group}} * [http://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E5%8D%97%E4%B8%8B%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4/ Minamishimo Kofun Group] – Yoshioka Town Board of Education {{Coord|36|26|22.47|N|139|0|34.00|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=title|name=Minamishimo Kofun Group}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|南下古墳群|version=103868454|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:OSM on jawiki]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic sites in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Yoshioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Minamishimo Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:南下古墳群]] [[Category:吉岡町の歴史]] [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Gunma Prefecture]]

While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''

revision.text:
[[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From Tomb C) from left to right: Tombs D, E, B, A, F}}]] [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From the east) from left to right: Tombs A, F, C, D, E}}]] {{Location map|Japan Gunma|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.47|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=34.00|label=Minamishimo Kofun Group|position=top|width=250|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Minamishimo Kofun Group}}}} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|26|24|N|139|0|33|E}} | zoom = 17 | float = right | width = 300| height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Minamishimo Kofun Group}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|22.45|N|139|0|34.03|E}} | label1=Tomb A | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|26|22.40|N|139|0|36.65|E}} | label2=Tomb B | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|26|24.00|N|139|0|29.30|E}} | label3=Tomb C | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|26|25.03|N|139|0|30.87|E}} | label4=Tomb D | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|26|24.40|N|139|0|32.30|E}} | label5=Tomb E | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 }} '''Minamishimo Kofun Group''' (南下古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Yoshioka, Gunma|Yoshioka, Gunma]], [[:en:Kitagunma_District,_Gunma|Kitagunma District]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. The site has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == The kofun group is situated on the southern slope of a small hill (Ōbayashi-yama) that lies east of Mount Haruna, where small rivers flowing into the Tone River intersect.Minamishimo Kofun Group Pamphlet. Once home to over 100 Kofuns, only nine survive today.Site explanation board. The burial chambers of five mounds (Tombs A, B, C, D, and E) remain open, all of which feature {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chambers). The stone chambers of Tombs A, B, and E are considered particularly significant, showcasing the evolution of stone construction from Kansai-style chambers (Tomb B) to the precise cutting techniques seen in Tombs A and E. Tombs A and E also retain traces of red construction guidelines on their walls, a rare feature found in only three tombs nationwide. The construction dates of the tombs are estimated to span from the mid-6th to late-7th centuries. The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''
(Ōbayashi Tomb 1)||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1318||Round mound||21 m diameter||colspan=3|Unknown||Unknown|| |} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Minamishimo Kofun Group – Designated September 8, 2023.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/uploaded/attachment/602455.pdf Gunma Prefecture Bulletin, September 8, 2023]}}. == Related While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''
(Ōbayashi Tomb 1)||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1318||Round mound||21 m diameter||colspan=3|Unknown||Unknown|| |} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Minamishimo Kofun Group – Designated September 8, 2023.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/uploaded/attachment/602455.pdf Gunma Prefecture Bulletin, September 8, 2023]}}. == Related Facilities == * Yoshioka Town Cultural Property Center (Yoshioka Town, Minamishimo) – Adjacent to the kofun group. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuya Kofun|ja|三津屋古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuya Kofun|qq=}} * Sōja Kofun Group ''' {{ill|Sōja Atagoyama Kofun|ja|総社愛宕山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja Atagoyama Kofun|qq=}} ''' [[:en:Hōtōzan Kofun|Hotoyama Kofun]] ''' [[:en:Jaketsuzan Kofun|Janakayama Kofun]] * {{ill|Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka Town)|lt=Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka Town)|lt=Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka, Gunma)|ja|三宮神社 (吉岡町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka, Gunma)|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minamishimo Kofun Group}} * [http://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E5%8D%97%E4%B8%8B%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4/ Minamishimo Kofun Group] – Yoshioka Town Board of Education {{Coord|36|26|22.47|N|139|0|34.00|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=title|name=Minamishimo Kofun Group}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|南下古墳群|version=103868454|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:OSM on jawiki]] [[Category:Yoshioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Minamishimo Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:南下古墳群]] [[Category:吉岡町の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Gunma Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Minamishimo Kofun Group}}

revision.text:
[[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From Tomb C) from left to right: Tombs D, E, B, A, F}}]] [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From the east) from left to right: Tombs A, F, C, D, E}}]] {{Location map|Japan Gunma|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.47|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=34.00|label=Minamishimo Kofun Group|position=top|width=250|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Minamishimo Kofun Group}}}} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|26|24|N|139|0|33|E}} | zoom = 17 | float = right | width = 300| height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Minamishimo Kofun Group}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|22.45|N|139|0|34.03|E}} | label1=Tomb A | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|26|22.40|N|139|0|36.65|E}} | label2=Tomb B | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|26|24.00|N|139|0|29.30|E}} | label3=Tomb C | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|26|25.03|N|139|0|30.87|E}} | label4=Tomb D | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|26|24.40|N|139|0|32.30|E}} | label5=Tomb E | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 }} '''Minamishimo Kofun Group''' (南下古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Yoshioka, Gunma|Yoshioka, Gunma]], [[:en:Kitagunma_District,_Gunma|Kitagunma District]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. The site has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == The kofun group is situated on the southern slope of a small hill (Ōbayashi-yama) that lies east of Mount Haruna, where small rivers flowing into the Tone River intersect.Minamishimo Kofun Group Pamphlet. Once home to over 100 Kofuns, only nine survive today.Site explanation board. The burial chambers of five mounds (Tombs A, B, C, D, and E) remain open, all of which feature {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chambers). The stone chambers of Tombs A, B, and E are considered particularly significant, showcasing the evolution of stone construction from Kansai-style chambers (Tomb B) to the precise cutting techniques seen in Tombs A and E. Tombs A and E also retain traces of red construction guidelines on their walls, a rare feature found in only three tombs nationwide. The construction dates of the tombs are estimated to span from the mid-6th to late-7th centuries. The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''
(Ōbayashi Tomb 1)||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1318||Round mound||21 m diameter||colspan=3|Unknown||Unknown|| |} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Minamishimo Kofun Group – Designated September 8, 2023.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/uploaded/attachment/602455.pdf Gunma Prefecture Bulletin, September 8, 2023]}}. == Related Facilities == * Yoshioka Town Cultural Property Center (Yoshioka Town, Minamishimo) – Adjacent to the kofun group. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuya Kofun|ja|三津屋古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuya Kofun|qq=}} * Sōja Kofun Group ''' {{ill|Sōja Atagoyama Kofun|ja|総社愛宕山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja Atagoyama Kofun|qq=}} ''' [[:en:Hōtōzan Kofun|Hotoyama Kofun]] ''' [[:en:Jaketsuzan Kofun|Janakayama Kofun]] * {{ill|Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka Town)|lt=Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka Town)|lt=Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka, Gunma)|ja|三宮神社 (吉岡町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka, Gunma)|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minamishimo Kofun Group}} * [http://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E5%8D%97%E4%B8%8B%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4/ Minamishimo Kofun Group] – Yoshioka Town Board of Education {{Coord|36|26|22.47|N|139|0|34.00|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=title|name=Minamishimo Kofun Group}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|南下古墳群|version=103868454|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:OSM on jawiki]] [[Category:Yoshioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Minamishimo Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:南下古墳群]] [[Category:吉岡町の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Gunma Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Minamishimo Kofun Group}}

The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''
(Ōbayashi Tomb 1)||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1318||Round mound||21 m diameter||colspan=3|Unknown||Unknown|| |} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Minamishimo Kofun Group – Designated September 8, 2023.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/uploaded/attachment/602455.pdf Gunma Prefecture Bulletin, September 8, 2023]}}. == Related Facilities == * Yoshioka Town Cultural Property Center (Yoshioka Town, Minamishimo) – Adjacent to the kofun group. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuya Kofun|ja|三津屋古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuya Kofun|qq=}} * Sōja Kofun Group ''' {{ill|Sōja Atagoyama Kofun|ja|総社愛宕山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja Atagoyama Kofun|qq=}} ''' [[:en:Hōtōzan Kofun|Hotoyama Kofun]] ''' [[:en:Jaketsuzan Kofun|Jaketsuzan Kofun]] * {{ill|Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka Town)|lt=Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka Town)|lt=Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka, Gunma)|ja|三宮神社 (吉岡町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka, Gunma)|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minamishimo Kofun Group}} * [http://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E5%8D%97%E4%B8%8B%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4/ Minamishimo Kofun Group] – Yoshioka Town Board of Education {{Coord|36|26|22.47|N|139|0|34.00|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=title|name=Minamishimo Kofun Group}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|南下古墳群|version=103868454|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Yoshioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Minamishimo Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:南下古墳群]] [[Category:吉岡町の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Gunma Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Minamishimo Kofun Group}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "町の歴史" to "の Municipal History"
revision.text:
[[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From Tomb C) from left to right: Tombs D, E, B, A, F}}]] [[File:Minamishimo Kofun Group, gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|310px|right|{{center|(From the east) from left to right: Tombs A, F, C, D, E}}]] {{Location map|Japan Gunma|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.47|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=34.00|label=Minamishimo Kofun Group|position=top|width=250|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Minamishimo Kofun Group}}}} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|26|24|N|139|0|33|E}} | zoom = 17 | float = right | width = 300| height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Minamishimo Kofun Group}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|22.45|N|139|0|34.03|E}} | label1=Tomb A | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=円墳.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|26|22.40|N|139|0|36.65|E}} | label2=Tomb B | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|26|24.00|N|139|0|29.30|E}} | label3=Tomb C | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=円墳.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|26|25.03|N|139|0|30.87|E}} | label4=Tomb D | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|26|24.40|N|139|0|32.30|E}} | label5=Tomb E | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=30 }} '''Minamishimo Kofun Group''' (南下古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Yoshioka, Gunma|Yoshioka, Gunma]], [[:en:Kitagunma_District,_Gunma|Kitagunma District]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. The site has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == The kofun group is situated on the southern slope of a small hill (Ōbayashi-yama) that lies east of Mount Haruna, where small rivers flowing into the Tone River intersect.Minamishimo Kofun Group Pamphlet. Once home to over 100 Kofuns, only nine survive today.Site explanation board. The burial chambers of five mounds (Tombs A, B, C, D, and E) remain open, all of which feature {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chambers). The stone chambers of Tombs A, B, and E are considered particularly significant, showcasing the evolution of stone construction from Kansai-style chambers (Tomb B) to the precise cutting techniques seen in Tombs A and E. Tombs A and E also retain traces of red construction guidelines on their walls, a rare feature found in only three tombs nationwide. The construction dates of the tombs are estimated to span from the mid-6th to late-7th centuries. The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''
(Ōbayashi Tomb 1)||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1318||Round mound||21 m diameter||colspan=3|Unknown||Unknown|| |} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Minamishimo Kofun Group – Designated September 8, 2023.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/uploaded/attachment/602455.pdf Gunma Prefecture Bulletin, September 8, 2023]}}. == Related Facilities == * Yoshioka Town Cultural Property Center (Yoshioka Town, Minamishimo) – Adjacent to the kofun group. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuya Kofun|ja|三津屋古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuya Kofun|qq=}} * Sōja Kofun Group ''' {{ill|Sōja Atagoyama Kofun|ja|総社愛宕山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja Atagoyama Kofun|qq=}} ''' [[:en:Hōtōzan Kofun|Hotoyama Kofun]] ''' [[:en:Jaketsuzan Kofun|Jaketsuzan Kofun]] * {{ill|Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka Town)|lt=Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka Town)|lt=Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka, Gunma)|ja|三宮神社 (吉岡町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka, Gunma)|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minamishimo Kofun Group}} * [http://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E5%8D%97%E4%B8%8B%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4/ Minamishimo Kofun Group] – Yoshioka Town Board of Education {{Coord|36|26|22.47|N|139|0|34.00|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=title|name=Minamishimo Kofun Group}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|南下古墳群|version=103868454|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Yoshioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Minamishimo Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:南下古墳群]] [[Category:吉岡の Municipal History]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Gunma Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Minamishimo Kofun Group}}

The chambers provide valuable insights into the evolution of stone construction and suggest relationships with the prominent Sōja Kofun Group in nearby Maebashi City. Six tombs (Tombs A–F) were designated as Gunma Prefecture Historic Sites in 2023. While the site is now open to the public as a historic park, entry into the chambers is restricted. == History of Preservation == * December 1, 1993: Tombs A and B designated as "Minamishimo Tomb A" and "Minamishimo Tomb B" by Yoshioka Town. * March 24, 2010: Tombs A–F designated as the "Minamishimo Kofun Group" by Yoshioka Town. * September 8, 2023: Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Tomb Details == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Minamishimo Kofun Group !rowspan=2|Tomb Name !rowspan=2|Location !rowspan=2|Shape !rowspan=2|Size !colspan=3|Stone Chamber !rowspan=2|Construction Period !rowspan=2|Notes |- !Structure !Total Length !Orientation |- |'''Tomb C'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1319||Round mound||15 m diameter||No-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||6.15 m||ESE||Mid to late 6th century||Located at the hilltop; cylindrical haniwa fragments found. |- |'''Tomb D'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1322-15||Round mound||13 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural stones)||5.63 m||SW||Early 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb B'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1326-2||Round mound||30 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (natural and cut stones)||7.62 m||SSW||Mid 7th century|| |- |'''Tomb A'''||Minamishimo Miyashiro 1315-1||Round mound||20–27 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||8 m or more||S||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines on the walls. |- |'''Tomb E'''||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1323-3||Round or square mound||17 m diameter||Double-sleeve horizontal chamber (precise cut stones)||4.43 m or more||SSW||Late 7th century||Features red construction guidelines. |- |'''Tomb F'''
(Ōbayashi Tomb 1)||Minamishimo Ōbayashi 1318||Round mound||21 m diameter||colspan=3|Unknown||Unknown|| |} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Minamishimo Kofun Group – Designated September 8, 2023.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/uploaded/attachment/602455.pdf Gunma Prefecture Bulletin, September 8, 2023]}}. == Related Facilities == * Yoshioka Town Cultural Property Center (Yoshioka Town, Minamishimo) – Adjacent to the kofun group. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mitsuya Kofun|ja|三津屋古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuya Kofun|qq=}} * Sōja Kofun Group ''' {{ill|Sōja Atagoyama Kofun|ja|総社愛宕山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja Atagoyama Kofun|qq=}} ''' [[:en:Hōtōzan Kofun|Hotoyama Kofun]] ''' [[:en:Jaketsuzan Kofun|Jaketsuzan Kofun]] * {{ill|Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka Town)|lt=Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka Town)|lt=Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka, Gunma)|ja|三宮神社 (吉岡町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka, Gunma)|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minamishimo Kofun Group}} * [http://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E5%8D%97%E4%B8%8B%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4/ Minamishimo Kofun Group] – Yoshioka Town Board of Education {{Coord|36|26|22.47|N|139|0|34.00|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=title|name=Minamishimo Kofun Group}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|南下古墳群|version=103868454|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Yoshioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Minamishimo Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:南下古墳群]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yoshioka]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Gunma Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Minamishimo Kofun Group}}
